1.1: The LORD spoke to Moses in the wilderness of Sinai, in the tent of meeting, on the first day of the second month in the second year after they came out of the land of Egypt, saying,
1.2: Take a census of the entire congregation of Israel, by their clans and their ancestral houses, counting every male by name, head by head.
1.3: Number all in Israel who are twenty years old or more and able to serve in the army. You and Aaron shall enroll them regiment by regiment.
1.4: With you shall stand one man from each tribe, each the head of his ancestral house.
1.5: These are the names of the men who shall stand with you: from Reuben, Elizur son of Shedeur;
1.6: from Simeon, Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai;
1.7: from Judah, Nahshon son of Amminadab;
1.8: from Issachar, Nethanel son of Zuar;
1.9: from Zebulun, Eliab son of Helon;
1.10: from the sons of Joseph: from Ephraim, Elishama son of Ammihud; from Manasseh, Gamaliel son of Pedahzur;
1.11: from Benjamin, Abidan son of Gideoni;
1.12: from Dan, Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai;
1.13: from Asher, Pagiel son of Ochran;
1.14: from Gad, Eliasaph son of Deuel;
1.15: from Naphtali, Ahira son of Enan.
1.16: These were the designated leaders of the congregation, the chiefs of their ancestral tribes, the heads of the clans of Israel.
1.17: Moses and Aaron took these men who had been designated by name,
1.18: and assembled the entire congregation on the first day of the second month. The people declared their ancestry by their clans and ancestral houses, and all males twenty years old and upward were counted one by one.
1.19: Just as the LORD had commanded Moses, so he counted them in the wilderness of Sinai.
1.20: The descendants of Reuben — Israel's firstborn — recorded by their families, by their ancestral houses, by the count of their names, every male twenty years and older who was able to serve in the army —
1.21: those enrolled from the tribe of Reuben were forty-six thousand five hundred.
1.22: From the descendants of Simeon, recorded by their families and ancestral houses, every male twenty years and older who was able to serve in the army —
1.23: those enrolled from the tribe of Simeon were fifty-nine thousand three hundred.
1.24: From the descendants of Gad, recorded by their families and ancestral houses, every male twenty years and older who was able to serve in the army —
1.25: those enrolled from the tribe of Gad were forty-five thousand six hundred and fifty.
1.26: From the descendants of Judah, recorded by their families and ancestral houses, every male twenty years and older who was able to serve in the army —
1.27: those enrolled from the tribe of Judah were seventy-four thousand six hundred.
1.28: From the descendants of Issachar, recorded by their families and ancestral houses, every male twenty years and older who was able to serve in the army —
1.29: those enrolled from the tribe of Issachar were fifty-four thousand four hundred.
1.30: From the descendants of Zebulun, recorded by their families and ancestral houses, every male twenty years and older who was able to serve in the army —
1.31: those enrolled from the tribe of Zebulun were fifty-seven thousand four hundred.
1.32: From the descendants of Joseph — the descendants of Ephraim — recorded by their families and ancestral houses, every male twenty years and older who was able to serve in the army —
1.33: those enrolled from the tribe of Ephraim were forty thousand five hundred.
1.34: From the descendants of Joseph — the descendants of Manasseh — recorded by their families and ancestral houses, every male twenty years and older who was able to serve in the army —
1.35: those enrolled from the tribe of Manasseh were thirty-two thousand two hundred.
1.36: From the descendants of Benjamin, recorded by their families and ancestral houses, every male twenty years and older who was able to serve in the army —
1.37: those enrolled from the tribe of Benjamin were thirty-five thousand four hundred.
1.38: From the descendants of Dan, recorded by their families and ancestral houses, every male twenty years and older who was able to serve in the army —
1.39: those enrolled from the tribe of Dan were sixty-two thousand seven hundred.
1.40: From the descendants of Asher, recorded by their families and ancestral houses, every male twenty years and older who was able to serve in the army —
1.41: those enrolled from the tribe of Asher were forty-one thousand five hundred.
1.42: From the descendants of Naphtali, throughout their generations, recorded by their families and ancestral houses, every male twenty years and older who was able to serve in the army —
1.43: those enrolled from the tribe of Naphtali were fifty-three thousand four hundred.
1.44: These are those enrolled — Moses and Aaron and the twelve leaders of Israel, one man per ancestral house, who conducted the enrollment.
1.45: All the Israelites twenty years and older who were able to serve in the army, enrolled by their ancestral houses —
1.46: the total enrolled was six hundred three thousand five hundred and fifty.
1.47: The Levites, however, were not enrolled among them by their ancestral tribe.
1.48: For the LORD had said to Moses,
1.49: Do not count the tribe of Levi or include them in the census of the Israelites.
1.50: Instead, appoint the Levites over the tabernacle of the testimony, over all its furnishings and all that belongs to it. They shall carry the tabernacle and all its vessels, they shall minister to it, and they shall camp around it.
1.51: When the tabernacle moves, the Levites shall take it apart; when it is to be set up, the Levites shall erect it. Any unauthorized person who comes near shall be put to death.
1.52: The Israelites shall camp each by his own company, each person under his own tribal standard throughout their regiments.
1.53: The Levites shall camp around the tabernacle of the testimony so that wrath does not fall on the congregation of Israel; the Levites shall keep watch over the tabernacle of the testimony.
1.54: The Israelites did everything the LORD had commanded Moses; so they did.

2.1: The LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron, saying,
2.2: The Israelites shall camp, each by his own standard and under the ensigns of his ancestral house, at a distance around the tent of meeting.
2.3: On the east side, toward the sunrise, the standard of the camp of Judah shall camp by their regiments. The leader of Judah is Nahshon son of Amminadab.
2.4: Those enrolled in his regiment were seventy-four thousand six hundred.
2.5: Camping next to them shall be the tribe of Issachar, with Nethanel son of Zuar as its leader.
2.6: Those enrolled were fifty-four thousand four hundred.
2.7: Then the tribe of Zebulun, with Eliab son of Helon as its leader.
2.8: Those enrolled were fifty-seven thousand four hundred.
2.9: The total enrolled in the camp of Judah was one hundred eighty-six thousand four hundred, by their regiments. They shall march first.
2.10: On the south side, the standard of the camp of Reuben shall be by their regiments. The leader of Reuben is Elizur son of Shedeur.
2.11: Those enrolled in his regiment were forty-six thousand five hundred.
2.12: Camping next to them shall be the tribe of Simeon, with Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai as its leader.
2.13: Those enrolled were fifty-nine thousand three hundred.
2.14: Then the tribe of Gad, with Eliasaph son of Reuel as its leader.
2.15: Those enrolled were forty-five thousand six hundred and fifty.
2.16: The total enrolled in the camp of Reuben was one hundred fifty-one thousand four hundred and fifty, by their regiments. They shall march second.
2.17: The tent of meeting shall then move, with the Levite camp in the middle. Each person in his position, under their standards — as they camp, so they shall march.
2.18: On the west side, the standard of the camp of Ephraim shall be by their regiments. The leader of Ephraim is Elishama son of Ammihud.
2.19: Those enrolled in his regiment were forty thousand five hundred.
2.20: Next to them shall be the tribe of Manasseh, with Gamaliel son of Pedahzur as its leader.
2.21: Those enrolled were thirty-two thousand two hundred.
2.22: Then the tribe of Benjamin, with Abidan son of Gideoni as its leader.
2.23: Those enrolled were thirty-five thousand four hundred.
2.24: The total enrolled in the camp of Ephraim was one hundred eight thousand one hundred, by their regiments. They shall march third.
2.25: On the north side, the standard of the camp of Dan shall be by their regiments. The leader of Dan is Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai.
2.26: Those enrolled in his regiment were sixty-two thousand seven hundred.
2.27: Camping next to them shall be the tribe of Asher, with Pagiel son of Ochran as its leader.
2.28: Those enrolled were forty-one thousand five hundred.
2.29: Then the tribe of Naphtali, with Ahira son of Enan as its leader.
2.30: Those enrolled were fifty-three thousand four hundred.
2.31: The total enrolled in the camp of Dan was one hundred fifty-seven thousand six hundred. They shall march last, by their standards.
2.32: These are the Israelites enrolled by their ancestral houses. The total enrolled in all the camps, by their regiments, was six hundred three thousand five hundred and fifty.
2.33: The Levites were not enrolled among the Israelites, as the LORD had commanded Moses.
2.34: The Israelites did everything the LORD had commanded Moses: they camped by their standards and they marched each by his clan, according to his ancestral house.

3.1: These are the descendants of Aaron and Moses at the time the LORD spoke with Moses on Mount Sinai.
3.2: The names of Aaron's sons were Nadab the firstborn, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.
3.3: These are the names of Aaron's sons, the anointed priests who were ordained to serve as priests.
3.4: Nadab and Abihu died before the LORD when they offered unauthorized fire before the LORD in the wilderness of Sinai; they had no sons. So Eleazar and Ithamar served as priests during the lifetime of Aaron their father.
3.6: Bring the tribe of Levi forward and present them to Aaron the priest, so they may serve him.
3.7: They shall fulfill the duties assigned to him and the entire congregation in front of the tent of meeting by performing the service of the tabernacle.
3.8: They shall be responsible for all the furnishings of the tent of meeting and fulfill the duties of the Israelites by performing the service of the tabernacle.
3.9: Give the Levites to Aaron and his sons; they are wholly given to him from among the Israelites.
3.10: Appoint Aaron and his sons to serve; they shall maintain their priestly duties. Any unauthorized person who comes near shall be put to death.
3.12: Behold, I have taken the Levites from among the Israelites as substitutes for every firstborn who opens the womb among the Israelites. The Levites shall be mine.
3.13: For every firstborn is mine; when I struck down all the firstborn in Egypt, I set apart for myself all the firstborn of Israel, human and animal alike. They are mine. I am the LORD.
3.14: The LORD spoke to Moses in the wilderness of Sinai, saying,
3.15: Count the Levites by their ancestral houses and families; count every male from a month old and upward.
3.16: So Moses counted them just as the LORD had directed.
3.17: The sons of Levi by name were Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.
3.18: The sons of Gershon by their clans were Libni and Shimei.
3.19: The sons of Kohath by their clans were Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel.
3.20: The sons of Merari by their clans were Mahli and Mushi. These are the Levite clans according to their ancestral houses.
3.21: From Gershon came the clan of Libni and the clan of Shimei; these are the Gershonite clans.
3.22: The total count of all males from a month old and upward was seven thousand five hundred.
3.23: The Gershonite clans shall camp behind the tabernacle, on the west side.
3.24: The head of the Gershonite ancestral house is Eliasaph son of Lael.
3.25: The Gershonites in the tent of meeting shall be responsible for the tabernacle, the tent with its covering, and the screen at the entrance to the tent of meeting,
3.26: the courtyard curtains and the screen at the entrance to the courtyard surrounding the tabernacle and the altar, and all the ropes — all the service involving these things.
3.27: From Kohath came the clan of Amram, the clan of Izhar, the clan of Hebron, and the clan of Uzziel; these are the Kohathite clans.
3.28: Counting all males from a month old and upward, there were eight thousand six hundred, responsible for the care of the sanctuary.
3.29: The Kohathite clans shall camp on the south side of the tabernacle.
3.30: The head of the Kohathite ancestral house is Elizaphan son of Uzziel.
3.31: They shall be responsible for the ark, the table, the lampstand, the altars, the vessels of the sanctuary used in worship, the curtain, and all the service involving these.
3.32: Eleazar son of Aaron the priest shall be chief over the leaders of the Levites, with oversight of those who keep the charge of the sanctuary.
3.33: From Merari came the clan of Mahli and the clan of Mushi; these are the Merarite clans.
3.34: The total count of all males from a month old and upward was six thousand two hundred.
3.35: The head of the Merarite ancestral house was Zuriel son of Abihail. They shall camp on the north side of the tabernacle.
3.36: The Merarites were responsible for the frames of the tabernacle, the bars, the pillars, the sockets, and all their accessories — all the service relating to these;
3.37: also the pillars of the surrounding courtyard with their sockets, pegs, and ropes.
3.38: Moses, Aaron, and his sons were to camp in front of the tabernacle, on the east side toward the sunrise. They were responsible for the sanctuary on behalf of the Israelites. Any unauthorized person who came near was to be put to death.
3.39: All the Levites Moses and Aaron counted at the LORD's command, by their clans — every male from a month old and upward — came to twenty-two thousand.
3.40: The LORD said to Moses, 'Count every firstborn male among the Israelites from a month old and upward, and record their names.'
3.41: Take the Levites for me — I am the LORD — in place of all the firstborn Israelites, and the Levites' livestock in place of all the firstborn livestock of the Israelites.
3.42: So Moses counted all the firstborn among the Israelites, as the LORD had commanded.
3.43: All the firstborn males registered by name from a month old and upward came to twenty-two thousand two hundred and seventy-three.
3.45: Take the Levites in place of all the firstborn Israelites, and the Levites' livestock in place of their livestock. The Levites are mine; I am the LORD.
3.46: For the redemption of the two hundred and seventy-three firstborn Israelites who exceed the number of Levites,
3.47: you shall collect five shekels per person, reckoned in the sanctuary shekel — the shekel being twenty gerahs.
3.48: Give the redemption money to Aaron and his sons.
3.49: So Moses took the redemption money from those who exceeded the number redeemed by the Levites.
3.50: From the firstborn Israelites he received one thousand three hundred and sixty-five shekels, by the sanctuary shekel.
3.51: Moses gave the redemption money to Aaron and his sons, just as the LORD had commanded.

4.1: The LORD said to Moses and Aaron,
4.2: Take a census of the Kohathites from among the Levites, by their clans and ancestral houses,
4.3: everyone from thirty to fifty years old who is qualified to perform service in the tent of meeting.
4.4: This is the work assigned to the Kohathites in the tent of meeting: the care of the most holy objects.
4.5: When the camp sets out, Aaron and his sons shall go in and take down the curtain of the screen and cover the ark of the testimony with it.
4.6: Over that they shall spread a covering of fine leather and place over it a blue cloth, then insert the carrying poles.
4.7: Over the table of the Presence they shall spread a blue cloth and place on it the plates, the dishes, the bowls, and the pitchers for the drink offering; the bread of the Presence shall remain on it.
4.8: They shall cover these with a scarlet cloth, then put over it a covering of fine leather, and insert its poles.
4.9: They shall take a blue cloth and cover the lampstand of the light, its lamps, its snuffers, its trays, and all the oil containers used to tend it,
4.10: and place it with all its utensils within a covering of fine leather, and put it on the carrying frame.
4.11: Over the golden altar they shall spread a blue cloth, cover it with fine leather, and insert its poles.
4.12: They shall take all the utensils of service used in the sanctuary, wrap them in a blue cloth, cover them with fine leather, and place them on the carrying frame.
4.13: They shall clear the ashes from the altar and spread a purple cloth over it.
4.14: They shall place on it all its utensils — the fire pans, the forks, the shovels, and the basins — all the altar vessels; then spread a covering of fine leather over it, and insert its poles.
4.15: When Aaron and his sons have finished covering the sanctuary and all its furnishings as the camp moves out, the Kohathites shall come to carry the load. But they must not touch the holy things, or they will die. These are the objects from the tent of meeting that the Kohathites shall carry.
4.16: Eleazar son of Aaron the priest shall be responsible for the oil of the light, the fragrant incense, the regular grain offering, and the anointing oil — overseeing the whole tabernacle and everything in it, the sanctuary and all its furnishings.
4.17: The LORD said to Moses and Aaron,
4.18: Do not let the Kohathite clan be cut off from among the Levites.
4.19: but do this for them so that they may live and not die when they come near the most holy things: Aaron and his sons shall go in and assign each person to his task and his load.
4.20: But the Kohathites themselves must not go in to look at the holy things even for a moment, or they will die.
4.22: Take a census of the Gershonites as well, by their ancestral houses and clans.
4.23: Count all those from thirty to fifty years old who qualify to serve and work in the tent of meeting.
4.24: This is the work assigned to the Gershonite clans: service and carrying.
4.25: They shall carry the curtains of the tabernacle and the tent of meeting, its covering and the leather covering over it, the screen at the entrance to the tent of meeting,
4.26: the courtyard hangings, the screen at the gate of the courtyard surrounding the tabernacle and the altar, their ropes, and all the equipment for this service. They shall do all the work relating to these items.
4.27: All the work of the Gershonites, in all their carrying and in all their service, shall be done under the direction of Aaron and his sons. You shall assign them the specific items they are to carry.
4.28: This is the work of the Gershonite clans in the tent of meeting; their supervision falls under Ithamar son of Aaron the priest.
4.29: As for the Merarites, count them by their clans and ancestral houses.
4.30: Count all from thirty to fifty years old who qualify to work in the tent of meeting.
4.31: This is what they are responsible to carry as their entire service in the tent of meeting: the frames of the tabernacle, its crossbars, its pillars, and its bases;
4.32: and the surrounding courtyard pillars with their bases, pegs, and ropes — all the equipment for this service. You shall assign each man by name to the specific items he is to carry.
4.33: This is the work of the Merarite clans, all their service in the tent of meeting, under the oversight of Ithamar son of Aaron the priest.
4.34: Moses, Aaron, and the leaders of the congregation counted the Kohathites by their clans and ancestral houses,
4.35: counting all from thirty to fifty years old who qualified to serve in the tent of meeting.
4.36: Those enrolled from their clans numbered two thousand seven hundred and fifty.
4.37: These are those enrolled from the Kohathite clans — all who served in the tent of meeting — whom Moses and Aaron counted according to the LORD's command given through Moses.
4.38: The Gershonites enrolled by their clans and ancestral houses —
4.39: all from thirty to fifty years old who qualified to work in the tent of meeting —
4.40: numbered two thousand six hundred and thirty.
4.41: These are those enrolled from the Gershonite clans — all who served in the tent of meeting — counted by Moses and Aaron at the LORD's command.
4.42: The Merarites enrolled by their clans and ancestral houses —
4.43: all from thirty to fifty years old who qualified to work in the tent of meeting —
4.45: These are those enrolled from the Merarite clans — counted by Moses and Aaron according to the LORD's command.
4.46: All the Levites whom Moses, Aaron, and the leaders of Israel enrolled, by their clans and ancestral houses —
4.47: from thirty to fifty years old, every man who could qualify to perform the service work and the carrying work in the tent of meeting —
4.48: numbered eight thousand five hundred and eighty.
4.49: Each was numbered by the LORD's command through Moses — each assigned to his service and his load. So they were counted, as the LORD commanded Moses.

5.2: Command the Israelites to send outside the camp every person with a skin disease, everyone with a bodily discharge, and everyone who has become unclean by contact with a corpse.
5.3: Send them out, both male and female, that they may not defile the camp where I dwell.
5.4: The Israelites did so and put them outside the camp, just as the LORD had spoken to Moses.
5.6: Say to the Israelites: When a man or woman commits any of the sins that people commit, acting in breach of faith against the LORD, that person is guilty,
5.7: that person shall confess the sin committed, make full restitution for the wrong, and add one-fifth of its value — giving it to the one who was wronged.
5.8: But if the wronged person has no relative to receive the restitution, then the restitution shall go to the LORD for the priest, in addition to the ram used to make atonement.
5.9: Every contribution — all the holy gifts that the Israelites bring to the priest — shall belong to the priest.
5.10: Each person's sacred gifts belong to that person; whatever one gives to the priest is the priest's.
5.12: Say to the Israelites: If a man's wife goes astray and acts unfaithfully against him,
5.13: if another man has lain with her, and it is hidden from her husband's knowledge, and she is not discovered even though she has defiled herself — there being no witness against her and she not caught in the act —
5.14: if a spirit of jealousy comes over the husband and he becomes suspicious of his wife who has defiled herself, or if a spirit of jealousy comes over him and he becomes suspicious of his wife when she has not defiled herself —
5.15: the man shall bring his wife to the priest and bring her offering — a tenth of an ephah of barley flour. He shall put no oil on it and add no frankincense, for it is a grain offering of jealousy — a grain offering of remembrance that calls sin to mind.
5.16: The priest shall bring her forward and present her before the LORD.
5.17: The priest shall take some holy water in a clay vessel and take some of the dust from the floor of the tabernacle and put it into the water.
5.18: The priest shall present the woman before the LORD, let down her hair, and place in her hands the grain offering of remembrance — the jealousy offering. The priest shall hold in his hand the bitter water that brings the curse.
5.19: The priest shall administer the oath to her: 'If no man has lain with you, and if you have not strayed into impurity while under your husband's authority, be free from this bitter water that brings the curse.'
5.20: But if you have gone astray while under your husband's authority, if you have defiled yourself and some man other than your husband has lain with you —
5.21: the priest shall invoke the curse upon the woman and say to her: 'May the LORD make you a source of cursing and an object of oath-swearing among your people, when the LORD causes your womb to miscarry and your abdomen to swell.'
5.22: May this water that brings the curse go into your body to cause your abdomen to swell and your womb to shrink.' And the woman shall say, 'Amen, Amen.'
5.23: The priest shall write these curses in a scroll and wash them off into the bitter water.
5.24: He shall make the woman drink the bitter water that brings the curse; the water that brings the curse shall enter her and cause bitterness.
5.25: Then the priest shall take the grain offering of jealousy from the woman's hand, wave it before the LORD, and bring it to the altar.
5.26: The priest shall take a handful of the grain offering as its memorial portion and burn it on the altar, and afterward make the woman drink the water.
5.27: When he has made her drink the water, if she has defiled herself and acted unfaithfully against her husband, the water that brings the curse shall cause bitter pain to enter her, her abdomen will swell, and her womb will waste away, and she will become a curse among her people.
5.28: But if the woman has not defiled herself and is clean, she will be free from harm and will be able to bear children.
5.29: This is the law for cases of jealousy, when a wife goes astray while under her husband's authority and defiles herself,
5.30: or when the spirit of jealousy comes over a man and he becomes jealous of his wife; then he shall bring the woman before the LORD, and the priest shall carry out all these procedures.
5.31: The man will be free of guilt, and the woman will bear her own iniquity.

6.2: Say to the Israelites: When a man or woman takes the special vow of a Nazirite, setting themselves apart for the LORD,
6.3: that person shall abstain from wine and beer, not drink any wine vinegar or beer vinegar, nor drink grape juice, nor eat fresh or dried grapes.
6.4: During the entire period of the Nazirite vow, that person shall not eat anything made from the grapevine, not even the seeds or the grape skins.
6.5: During the entire period of the Nazirite vow, no razor shall touch his head. The Nazirite is holy to the LORD until the days of his separation are complete; he shall let the hair of his head grow freely.
6.6: Throughout the period of separation, the Nazirite shall not go near a dead body.
6.7: Even if his father, mother, brother, or sister dies, he shall not defile himself, because the sign of God's consecration is on his head.
6.8: The Nazirite is holy to the LORD throughout the entire period of separation.
6.9: If someone dies suddenly in his presence and he defiles his consecrated head, he shall shave his head on the day of his cleansing — the seventh day.
6.10: On the eighth day he shall bring to the priest at the entrance of the tent of meeting two turtledoves or two young pigeons.
6.11: The priest shall offer one as a sin offering and the other as a burnt offering and make atonement for him, because he became unclean by contact with the dead. He shall re-consecrate his head that same day.
6.12: He shall devote himself again to the LORD for the full term of his separation and bring a year-old male lamb as a guilt offering. The earlier days shall not count, because his separation was defiled.
6.13: This is the law of the Nazirite: when the days of his separation are complete, he shall be brought to the entrance of the tent of meeting.
6.14: He shall present his offering to the LORD: one unblemished year-old male lamb for a burnt offering, one unblemished year-old female lamb for a sin offering, and one unblemished ram for a fellowship offering,
6.15: along with a basket of unleavened bread — fine flour loaves mixed with oil and unleavened wafers brushed with oil — and their accompanying grain offerings and drink offerings.
6.16: The priest shall bring these before the LORD and sacrifice his sin offering and burnt offering.
6.17: He shall present the ram as a fellowship offering to the LORD, along with the basket of unleavened bread; the priest shall also offer its grain offering and drink offering.
6.18: The Nazirite shall shave his consecrated head at the entrance of the tent of meeting and take the hair from his consecrated head and put it on the fire under the fellowship offering.
6.19: The priest shall take the boiled shoulder of the ram, one unleavened loaf from the basket, and one unleavened wafer, and place them in the hands of the Nazirite after he has shaved his consecrated head.
6.20: The priest shall wave them as a wave offering before the LORD. This is a holy portion for the priest, along with the breast of the wave offering and the thigh of the contribution offering. After this, the Nazirite may drink wine.
6.21: This is the law of the Nazirite who makes a vow. He shall bring to the LORD the offering required by his Nazirite vow, in addition to whatever else he can afford. In accordance with his vow, he shall carry out everything required by the law of his separation.
6.23: Tell Aaron and his sons: This is how you are to bless the Israelites. Say to them:
6.24: May the LORD bless you and keep you;
6.25: may the LORD make his face shine on you and be gracious to you;
6.26: may the LORD lift up his face toward you and grant you peace.
6.27: This is how they will put my name on the Israelites, and I will bless them.

7.1: On the day Moses finished setting up the tabernacle, he anointed and consecrated it together with all its furnishings, and anointed and consecrated the altar and all its utensils.
7.2: the leaders of Israel — heads of their ancestral houses, leaders of the tribes, those who had overseen the census — drew near
7.3: They brought their offering before the LORD — six covered carts and twelve oxen: one cart for every two leaders and one ox for each leader — and presented them before the tabernacle.
7.4: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
7.5: Accept these from them for the work of the tent of meeting, and give them to the Levites — each man a portion according to the service he performs.
7.6: Moses took the carts and the oxen and distributed them to the Levites.
7.7: Two carts and four oxen he gave to the sons of Gershon, proportioned to their service.
7.8: Four carts and eight oxen he gave to the sons of Merari, according to their service, under the supervision of Ithamar son of Aaron the priest.
7.9: But he gave nothing to the sons of Kohath, because their duty was to carry the holy things, which had to be borne on their shoulders.
7.10: The leaders brought dedication offerings for the altar on the day it was anointed; the leaders presented their offering before the altar.
7.11: The LORD said to Moses: Each leader shall present his offering on his own day for the dedication of the altar.
7.12: The one who offered his offering on the first day was Nahshon son of Amminadab, of the tribe of Judah.
7.13: His offering was one silver dish weighing 130 shekels and one silver basin of 70 shekels, by the sanctuary shekel — both filled with fine flour mixed with oil as a grain offering;
7.14: one gold pan of 10 shekels, full of incense;
7.15: one bull, one ram, one yearling male lamb — for a burnt offering;
7.16: one male goat — for a sin offering;
7.17: and for the fellowship offering: two oxen, five rams, five male goats, five yearling lambs. This was the offering of Nahshon son of Amminadab.
7.18: On the second day Nethaneel son of Zuar, leader of Issachar, offered his offering.
7.19: His offering was one silver dish weighing 130 shekels and one silver basin of 70 shekels, by the sanctuary shekel — both filled with fine flour mixed with oil as a grain offering;
7.20: one gold pan of 10 shekels, full of incense;
7.21: one bull, one ram, one yearling male lamb — for a burnt offering;
7.22: one male goat — for a sin offering;
7.23: and for the fellowship offering: two oxen, five rams, five male goats, five yearling lambs. This was the offering of Nethaneel son of Zuar.
7.24: On the third day Eliab son of Helon, leader of Zebulun, offered his offering.
7.25: His offering was one silver dish weighing 130 shekels and one silver basin of 70 shekels, by the sanctuary shekel — both filled with fine flour mixed with oil as a grain offering;
7.26: one gold pan of 10 shekels, full of incense;
7.27: one bull, one ram, one yearling male lamb — for a burnt offering;
7.28: one male goat — for a sin offering;
7.29: and for the fellowship offering: two oxen, five rams, five male goats, five yearling lambs. This was the offering of Eliab son of Helon.
7.30: On the fourth day Elizur son of Shedeur, leader of Reuben, offered his offering.
7.31: His offering was one silver dish weighing 130 shekels and one silver basin of 70 shekels, by the sanctuary shekel — both filled with fine flour mixed with oil as a grain offering;
7.32: one gold pan of 10 shekels, full of incense;
7.33: one bull, one ram, one yearling male lamb — for a burnt offering;
7.34: one male goat — for a sin offering;
7.35: and for the fellowship offering: two oxen, five rams, five male goats, five yearling lambs. This was the offering of Elizur son of Shedeur.
7.36: On the fifth day Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai, leader of Simeon, offered his offering.
7.37: His offering was one silver dish weighing 130 shekels and one silver basin of 70 shekels, by the sanctuary shekel — both filled with fine flour mixed with oil as a grain offering;
7.38: one gold pan of 10 shekels, full of incense;
7.39: one bull, one ram, one yearling male lamb — for a burnt offering;
7.40: one male goat — for a sin offering;
7.41: and for the fellowship offering: two oxen, five rams, five male goats, five yearling lambs. This was the offering of Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai.
7.42: On the sixth day Eliasaph son of Deuel, leader of Gad, offered his offering.
7.43: His offering was one silver dish weighing 130 shekels and one silver basin of 70 shekels, by the sanctuary shekel — both filled with fine flour mixed with oil as a grain offering;
7.44: one gold pan of 10 shekels, full of incense;
7.45: one bull, one ram, one yearling male lamb — for a burnt offering;
7.46: one male goat — for a sin offering;
7.47: and for the fellowship offering: two oxen, five rams, five male goats, five yearling lambs. This was the offering of Eliasaph son of Deuel.
7.48: On the seventh day Elishama son of Ammihud, leader of Ephraim, offered his offering.
7.49: His offering was one silver dish weighing 130 shekels and one silver basin of 70 shekels, by the sanctuary shekel — both filled with fine flour mixed with oil as a grain offering;
7.50: one gold pan of 10 shekels, full of incense;
7.51: one bull, one ram, one yearling male lamb — for a burnt offering;
7.52: one male goat — for a sin offering;
7.53: and for the fellowship offering: two oxen, five rams, five male goats, five yearling lambs. This was the offering of Elishama son of Ammihud.
7.54: On the eighth day Gamaliel son of Pedahzur, leader of Manasseh, offered his offering.
7.55: His offering was one silver dish weighing 130 shekels and one silver basin of 70 shekels, by the sanctuary shekel — both filled with fine flour mixed with oil as a grain offering;
7.56: one gold pan of 10 shekels, full of incense;
7.57: one bull, one ram, one yearling male lamb — for a burnt offering;
7.58: one male goat — for a sin offering;
7.59: and for the fellowship offering: two oxen, five rams, five male goats, five yearling lambs. This was the offering of Gamaliel son of Pedahzur.
7.60: On the ninth day Abidan son of Gideoni, leader of Benjamin, offered his offering.
7.61: His offering was one silver dish weighing 130 shekels and one silver basin of 70 shekels, by the sanctuary shekel — both filled with fine flour mixed with oil as a grain offering;
7.62: one gold pan of 10 shekels, full of incense;
7.63: one bull, one ram, one yearling male lamb — for a burnt offering;
7.64: one male goat — for a sin offering;
7.65: and for the fellowship offering: two oxen, five rams, five male goats, five yearling lambs. This was the offering of Abidan son of Gideoni.
7.66: On the tenth day Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai, leader of Dan, offered his offering.
7.67: His offering was one silver dish weighing 130 shekels and one silver basin of 70 shekels, by the sanctuary shekel — both filled with fine flour mixed with oil as a grain offering;
7.68: one gold pan of 10 shekels, full of incense;
7.69: one bull, one ram, one yearling male lamb — for a burnt offering;
7.70: one male goat — for a sin offering;
7.71: and for the fellowship offering: two oxen, five rams, five male goats, five yearling lambs. This was the offering of Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai.
7.72: On the eleventh day Pagiel son of Ocran, leader of Asher, offered his offering.
7.73: His offering was one silver dish weighing 130 shekels and one silver basin of 70 shekels, by the sanctuary shekel — both filled with fine flour mixed with oil as a grain offering;
7.74: one gold pan of 10 shekels, full of incense;
7.75: one bull, one ram, one yearling male lamb — for a burnt offering;
7.76: one male goat — for a sin offering;
7.77: and for the fellowship offering: two oxen, five rams, five male goats, five yearling lambs. This was the offering of Pagiel son of Ocran.
7.78: On the twelfth day Ahira son of Enan, leader of Naphtali, offered his offering.
7.79: His offering was one silver dish weighing 130 shekels and one silver basin of 70 shekels, by the sanctuary shekel — both filled with fine flour mixed with oil as a grain offering;
7.80: one gold pan of 10 shekels, full of incense;
7.81: one bull, one ram, one yearling male lamb — for a burnt offering;
7.82: one male goat — for a sin offering;
7.83: and for the fellowship offering: two oxen, five rams, five male goats, five yearling lambs. This was the offering of Ahira son of Enan.
7.84: This was the dedication offering for the altar at its anointing, brought by the leaders of Israel: twelve silver dishes, twelve silver basins, twelve gold pans.
7.85: Each silver dish weighed 130 shekels and each basin 70. All the silver vessels together: 2,400 shekels by the sanctuary shekel.
7.86: The twelve gold pans, each full of incense and weighing 10 shekels by the sanctuary shekel — all the gold of the pans amounted to 120 shekels.
7.87: All the animals for the burnt offering: twelve bulls, twelve rams, twelve yearling male lambs with their grain offerings. For the sin offering: twelve male goats.
7.88: All the animals for the fellowship offering: twenty-four bulls, sixty rams, sixty male goats, sixty yearling lambs. This was the dedication offering for the altar after it was anointed.
7.89: When Moses went into the tent of meeting to speak with the LORD, he heard the voice speaking to him from above the atonement cover that was on the ark of the covenant document, from between the two cherubim. And the LORD spoke to him.

8.1: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
8.2: Speak to Aaron and tell him: When you set up the lamps, the seven lamps are to light the space in front of the lampstand.
8.3: Aaron did so; he set up the lamps to face the front of the lampstand, as the LORD had commanded Moses.
8.4: The lampstand was made of hammered gold from its base to its blossoms — all of beaten work, made according to the pattern the LORD had shown Moses.
8.5: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
8.6: Take the Levites from among the Israelites and cleanse them.
8.7: This is how you shall cleanse them: sprinkle purification water on them; have them shave their whole body, wash their clothes, and so make themselves clean.
8.8: Then they shall take a young bull with its grain offering of fine flour mixed with oil, and you shall take a second young bull for a sin offering.
8.9: You shall bring the Levites before the tent of meeting and assemble the whole community of Israel.
8.10: You shall present the Levites before the LORD, and the Israelites shall lay their hands on the Levites.
8.11: and Aaron shall present the Levites before the LORD as a wave offering from the Israelites, so that they may carry out the LORD's service.
8.12: The Levites shall lay their hands on the heads of the bulls; and you shall offer one as a sin offering and the other as a burnt offering to the LORD, to make atonement for the Levites.
8.13: You shall station the Levites before Aaron and his sons and present them as a wave offering to the LORD.
8.14: In this way you shall set the Levites apart from the Israelites, and the Levites shall be mine.
8.15: After this, the Levites shall come to serve at the tent of meeting. You shall cleanse them and present them as a wave offering.
8.16: For they are wholly given to me from among the Israelites. I have taken them for myself in place of all who first open the womb — in place of every firstborn among the Israelites.
8.17: For every firstborn among the Israelites is mine, both human and animal. On the day I struck every firstborn in Egypt I set them apart for myself.
8.18: And I have taken the Levites in place of all the firstborn among the Israelites.
8.19: I have given the Levites as a gift to Aaron and his sons from among the Israelites, to serve on behalf of the Israelites at the tent of meeting and to make atonement for them, so that no plague strikes the Israelites when they approach the sanctuary.
8.20: Moses, Aaron, and the whole community of Israel did to the Levites everything the LORD commanded Moses about them. The Israelites did this to the Levites.
8.21: The Levites purified themselves and washed their clothes. Aaron presented them as a wave offering before the LORD and made atonement for them to cleanse them.
8.22: After this the Levites went in to perform their service at the tent of meeting, before Aaron and his sons, just as the LORD had commanded Moses about the Levites — so it was done.
8.23: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
8.24: This is what applies to the Levites: from twenty-five years old and upward they shall enter to serve in the work of the tent of meeting.
8.25: And from age fifty they shall retire from the service work and serve no longer.
8.26: They may assist their fellow Levites at the tent of meeting by keeping the charge, but they shall perform no heavy work. This is how you shall assign the Levites' duties.

9.1: The LORD spoke to Moses in the wilderness of Sinai, in the first month of the second year after the exodus from Egypt, saying,
9.2: Let the Israelites keep the Passover at its appointed time.
9.3: On the fourteenth day of this month, at twilight, you shall keep it at its appointed time, following all its statutes and all its ordinances.
9.4: Moses told the Israelites to keep the Passover.
9.5: They kept the Passover on the fourteenth day of the first month at twilight in the wilderness of Sinai. The Israelites did everything the LORD had commanded Moses.
9.6: But there were some men who were ceremonially unclean because of a human corpse and could not keep the Passover on that day. They came before Moses and Aaron that day
9.7: and said to him, We are unclean because of a corpse. Why should we be excluded from bringing the LORD's offering at its appointed time among the Israelites?
9.8: Moses said to them, Wait here, and I will hear what the LORD commands about you.
9.9: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
9.10: Tell the Israelites: If any of you or your descendants is unclean because of a corpse, or is away on a long journey, he may still keep the Passover to the LORD.
9.11: They shall keep it on the fourteenth day of the second month at twilight, eating it with unleavened bread and bitter herbs.
9.12: They shall leave none of it until morning and shall not break any of its bones. They shall observe it according to all the statutes of the Passover.
9.13: But the man who is clean and not on a journey and yet fails to keep the Passover — that person shall be cut off from among his people. Because he did not bring the LORD's offering at the appointed time, that man shall bear his guilt.
9.14: If a foreigner residing among you wants to keep the Passover to the LORD, he must do so according to the statutes and ordinances of the Passover. You shall have one statute, both for the resident alien and for the native-born.
9.15: On the day the tabernacle was set up, the cloud covered the tabernacle — the tent of the covenant document — and at evening it appeared over the tabernacle like fire until morning.
9.16: This was the regular pattern: the cloud covered it by day and the appearance of fire by night.
9.17: Whenever the cloud lifted from above the tabernacle, the Israelites set out. And wherever the cloud settled, there the Israelites camped.
9.18: At the LORD's command the Israelites set out, and at the LORD's command they camped. As long as the cloud rested over the tabernacle, they stayed in camp.
9.19: When the cloud remained over the tabernacle for many days, the Israelites kept the LORD's charge and did not set out.
9.20: Sometimes the cloud remained over the tabernacle for only a few days; at the LORD's command they remained in camp, and at the LORD's command they set out.
9.21: Sometimes the cloud remained only from evening until morning; when the cloud lifted in the morning, they set out. Whether by day or by night the cloud lifted, they set out.
9.22: Whether the cloud remained for two days, a month, or a year over the tabernacle, the Israelites stayed in camp and did not set out. But when it lifted, they set out.
9.23: At the LORD's command they camped, and at the LORD's command they set out. They kept the LORD's charge at the LORD's command through Moses.

10.1: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
10.2: Make two silver trumpets; make them of hammered silver. They shall serve to summon the assembly and to signal the camps to set out.
10.3: When both are blown, the whole assembly shall gather to you at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
10.4: If only one is blown, only the leaders — the heads of Israel's thousands — shall gather to you.
10.5: When you blow an alarm, the camps on the east side shall set out.
10.6: When you blow an alarm a second time, the camps on the south side shall set out. An alarm shall be sounded for each journey.
10.7: But when the assembly is to be gathered, you shall blow without an alarm call.
10.8: The priests, the sons of Aaron, shall blow the trumpets. This shall be a permanent statute throughout your generations.
10.9: When you go to war in your land against an enemy who attacks you, sound an alarm with the trumpets. You will be remembered before the LORD your God and saved from your enemies.
10.10: On your joyful days — your appointed festivals and the beginnings of your months — you shall blow the trumpets over your burnt offerings and your fellowship offerings. They will be a reminder before your God. I am the LORD your God.
10.11: On the twentieth day of the second month of the second year, the cloud lifted from above the tabernacle of the covenant.
10.12: The Israelites set out from the wilderness of Sinai, stage by stage, and the cloud came to rest in the wilderness of Paran.
10.13: They set out for the first time at the LORD's command through Moses.
10.14: The standard of the camp of Judah set out first, by their regiments, with Nahshon son of Amminadab commanding their force.
10.15: Over the force of the tribe of Issachar was Nethaneel son of Zuar.
10.16: Over the force of the tribe of Zebulun was Eliab son of Helon.
10.17: The tabernacle was taken down, and the sons of Gershon and the sons of Merari set out, carrying the tabernacle.
10.18: The standard of the camp of Reuben set out by their regiments, with Elizur son of Shedeur commanding their force.
10.19: Over the force of the tribe of Simeon was Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai.
10.20: Over the force of the tribe of Gad was Eliasaph son of Deuel.
10.21: The Kohathites set out, carrying the holy objects. The others would set up the tabernacle before they arrived.
10.22: The standard of the camp of Ephraim set out by their regiments, with Elishama son of Ammihud commanding their force.
10.23: Over the force of the tribe of Manasseh was Gamaliel son of Pedahzur.
10.24: Over the force of the tribe of Benjamin was Abidan son of Gideoni.
10.25: The standard of the camp of Dan set out — the rear guard of all the camps, by their regiments — with Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai commanding their force.
10.26: Over the force of the tribe of Asher was Pagiel son of Ocran.
10.27: Over the force of the tribe of Naphtali was Ahira son of Enan.
10.28: These were the marching orders of the Israelites, by their regiments, when they set out.
10.29: Moses said to Hobab son of Raguel the Midianite, Moses' father-in-law: We are setting out for the place the LORD said he would give us. Come with us and we will treat you well, for the LORD has promised good things to Israel.
10.30: But he said to him, I will not go; I will return to my own land and my own people.
10.31: Moses said, Please do not leave us, for you know how we are to camp in the wilderness, and you will serve as eyes for us.
10.32: And if you come with us, whatever good the LORD does for us, we will share with you.
10.33: They set out from the mountain of the LORD on a three days' journey, with the ark of the LORD's covenant going before them on those three days to find a resting place for them.
10.34: The cloud of the LORD was over them by day when they set out from the camp.
10.35: Whenever the ark set out, Moses said: Rise up, LORD! Let your enemies be scattered; let those who hate you flee before you!
10.36: And when it came to rest, he would say: Return, O LORD, to the countless thousands of Israel.

11.1: The people complained in the hearing of the LORD about their hardships. When the LORD heard it, his anger was kindled, and fire from the LORD burned among them and consumed some who were at the edge of the camp.
11.2: The people cried out to Moses, and when Moses prayed to the LORD, the fire died down.
11.3: So that place was called Taberah, because fire from the LORD had burned among them.
11.4: The mixed multitude among them had a strong craving, and the Israelites also wept again and said, Who will give us meat to eat?
11.5: We remember the fish we ate in Egypt free of charge — the cucumbers, the melons, the leeks, the onions, the garlic.
11.6: But now our appetite has withered; there is nothing at all to look at except this manna.
11.7: The manna was like coriander seed and its appearance like the appearance of bdellium resin.
11.8: The people went around gathering it; they ground it in mills or beat it in mortars, then cooked it in pots and made loaves from it. It tasted like rich oil.
11.9: When the dew settled on the camp in the night, the manna settled with it.
11.10: Moses heard the people weeping throughout their clans, everyone at the door of his tent. The anger of the LORD blazed hot, and Moses was also distressed.
11.11: Moses said to the LORD: Why have you brought this trouble on your servant? Why have I not found favor in your sight, that you lay the burden of all this people on me?
11.12: Did I conceive all this people? Did I give birth to them, that you should say to me, Carry them in your arms, as a nursing father carries a nursing child, to the land you swore to their ancestors?
11.13: Where am I to get meat to give to all this people? For they weep before me and say, Give us meat to eat.
11.14: I am not able to carry all this people alone; it is too heavy for me.
11.15: If this is how you are going to treat me, kill me right now — if I have found favor in your sight — and do not let me face my own ruin.
11.16: The LORD said to Moses: Gather for me seventy men from the elders of Israel — men you know to be elders and officers of the people — and bring them to the tent of meeting, so that they may stand there with you.
11.17: I will come down and speak with you there. I will take some of the spirit that is on you and put it on them. They will share the burden of the people with you so that you do not carry it alone.
11.18: And say to the people: Consecrate yourselves for tomorrow, and you will eat meat. For you have wept in the LORD's hearing and said, Who will give us meat to eat? It was better for us in Egypt. Therefore the LORD will give you meat, and you will eat.
11.19: You will not eat it for just one day, or two days, or five days, or ten days, or twenty days,
11.20: but a whole month, until it comes out of your nostrils and becomes repulsive to you — because you have rejected the LORD who is among you and have wept before him, saying, Why did we ever leave Egypt?
11.21: But Moses said: The people I am among number six hundred thousand foot soldiers, and you have said, I will give them meat to eat for a whole month!
11.22: Would enough flocks and herds be slaughtered to provide for them? Would all the fish of the sea be gathered together to provide for them?
11.23: The LORD said to Moses: Is the LORD's hand too short? Now you will see whether my word comes true for you or not.
11.24: Moses went out and told the people the LORD's words, then gathered seventy of the elders of the people and positioned them around the tent.
11.25: Then the LORD came down in the cloud and spoke to him. He took some of the spirit that was on Moses and gave it to the seventy elders. When the spirit rested on them, they prophesied — but they did so only that once.
11.26: Two men had remained in the camp — one named Eldad and the other Medad. The spirit rested on them as well; they were among those registered but had not gone out to the tent. And they prophesied in the camp.
11.27: A young man ran and told Moses: Eldad and Medad are prophesying in the camp!
11.28: Joshua son of Nun, who had been Moses' assistant since his youth, said: My lord Moses, stop them!
11.29: Moses said to him: Are you jealous on my behalf? Would that all the LORD's people were prophets and that the LORD would put his spirit on them!
11.30: Then Moses and the elders of Israel returned to the camp.
11.31: Then a wind went out from the LORD and drove quails from the sea. It dropped them beside the camp — a day's journey in every direction around the camp, about three feet deep on the surface of the ground.
11.32: The people spent all that day, all that night, and all the next day gathering quails. The least any one gathered was ten homers. They spread them out around the camp to dry.
11.33: While the meat was still between their teeth, before it was chewed, the anger of the LORD blazed against the people, and the LORD struck the people with a very great plague.
11.34: So that place was called Kibroth-hattaavah — the Graves of Craving — because there they buried the people who had craved.
11.35: From Kibroth-hattaavah the people traveled to Hazeroth, and they stayed at Hazeroth.

12.1: Miriam and Aaron spoke against Moses because of the Cushite woman he had married — for he had married a Cushite woman.
12.2: They said: Has the LORD spoken only through Moses? Has he not also spoken through us? And the LORD heard it.
12.3: Now Moses was a very humble man, more humble than anyone else on the face of the earth.
12.4: At once the LORD said to Moses, Aaron, and Miriam: Come out, you three, to the tent of meeting. And the three of them came out.
12.5: The LORD came down in a pillar of cloud and stood at the entrance of the tent, and called Aaron and Miriam. Both of them stepped forward.
12.6: He said: Hear my words. When there is a prophet among you, I the LORD make myself known to him in a vision; I speak with him in a dream.
12.7: Not so with my servant Moses. He is faithful in all my house.
12.8: With him I speak mouth to mouth, clearly and not in riddles, and he sees the form of the LORD. Why then were you not afraid to speak against my servant Moses?
12.9: The anger of the LORD burned against them, and he departed.
12.10: When the cloud withdrew from over the tent, Miriam was leprous — white as snow. Aaron turned toward Miriam, and she was leprous.
12.11: Aaron said to Moses: Oh, my lord, please do not lay on us the guilt of the sin we have foolishly committed.
12.12: Do not let her be like one stillborn, whose flesh is half eaten away when it comes out of the womb.
12.13: Moses cried out to the LORD: O God, please heal her!
12.14: The LORD said to Moses: If her father had merely spit in her face, would she not be in disgrace for seven days? Let her be confined outside the camp for seven days; after that she may be brought back in.
12.15: So Miriam was confined outside the camp for seven days, and the people did not set out until Miriam was brought back in.
12.16: After that the people moved from Hazeroth and camped in the wilderness of Paran.

31.1: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
31.2: "Take full vengeance for the Israelites on the Midianites; after that you will be gathered to your people."
31.3: Moses said to the people, "Arm men from among you for war so that they may march against Midian and carry out the LORD's vengeance."
31.4: Send one thousand from each tribe of Israel — one thousand per tribe — to the war.
31.5: Out of Israel's thousands, one thousand from each tribe were drafted — twelve thousand troops armed for war.
31.6: Moses dispatched them — a thousand from each tribe — with Phinehas son of Eleazar the priest, who carried the sacred vessels and the alarm trumpets.
31.7: They waged war against Midian, as the LORD commanded Moses, and killed every male.
31.8: Among those killed were the five kings of Midian: Evi, Rekem, Zur, Hur, and Reba. They also killed Balaam son of Beor with the sword.
31.9: The Israelites took the Midianite women and children captive and seized all their livestock, flocks, and goods as plunder.
31.10: All their settled cities and all their encampments they burned to the ground.
31.11: They gathered all the plunder — both human captives and animals.
31.12: They brought the captives, plunder, and spoil to Moses, Eleazar the priest, and the whole Israelite congregation at the camp on the plains of Moab, beside the Jordan near Jericho.
31.13: Moses, Eleazar the priest, and all the community leaders went out to meet the army outside the camp.
31.14: But Moses was furious with the military officers, the commanders of thousands and of hundreds, who had returned from the campaign.
31.15: Moses said to them, "Have you let all the women live?"
31.16: "These very women, following Balaam's advice, led the Israelites to betray the LORD in the matter of Peor — and that brought the plague on the LORD's congregation."
31.17: "Now kill every male child, and kill every woman who has had sexual relations with a man."
31.18: "But all the young girls who have not had relations with a man, keep alive for yourselves."
31.19: "You must remain outside the camp for seven days. Anyone who has killed or touched a corpse must purify themselves and their captives on the third day and on the seventh day."
31.20: "Purify every garment, every article of leather, everything made of goats' hair, and every wooden article."
31.21: Eleazar the priest said to the soldiers who had gone to war, "This is the ordinance the LORD commanded through Moses:"
31.22: "Gold, silver, bronze, iron, tin, and lead —"
31.23: "— everything that can withstand fire must be passed through fire and will then be clean; but it must also be rinsed with the purification water. Whatever fire would destroy must be passed through water."
31.24: "Wash your clothing on the seventh day, and you will be clean; then you may enter the camp."
31.25: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
31.26: "Take a full count of all the plunder captured, both persons and animals — you, Eleazar the priest, and the tribal leaders of the congregation."
31.27: "Divide the plunder equally between the warriors who fought and the rest of the congregation."
31.28: "From the warrior's half, levy a tribute for the LORD: one in five hundred from the persons, cattle, donkeys, and sheep."
31.29: "Take it from their share and present it to Eleazar the priest as a contribution to the LORD."
31.30: "From the Israelites' half, take one out of every fifty — from persons, cattle, donkeys, and flocks, every kind of animal — and give them to the Levites who maintain the LORD's tabernacle."
31.31: Moses and Eleazar the priest did exactly as the LORD commanded Moses.
31.32: The remaining plunder the fighting men had taken came to six hundred seventy-five thousand sheep,
31.35: and thirty-two thousand young women who had not had relations with a man.
31.36: The warrior's half: three hundred thirty-seven thousand five hundred sheep;
31.37: the LORD's tribute from the sheep, six hundred seventy-five;
31.38: the cattle were thirty-six thousand, with the LORD's tribute being seventy-two;
31.39: the donkeys were thirty thousand five hundred, with the LORD's tribute being sixty-one;
31.40: the persons were sixteen thousand, with the LORD's tribute being thirty-two.
31.41: Moses gave the tribute — the LORD's contribution — to Eleazar the priest, as the LORD had commanded.
31.42: From the Israelites' half — which Moses had separated from the fighting men's share —
31.43: the congregation received three hundred thirty-seven thousand five hundred sheep,
31.47: From the Israelites' half, Moses took one out of every fifty — persons and animals alike — and gave them to the Levites who maintained the LORD's tabernacle, as the LORD had commanded.
31.48: The military officers — commanders of thousands and of hundreds — came forward to Moses
31.49: and said, "We have tallied every soldier under our command, and not one man is unaccounted for."
31.50: "So we have brought an offering to the LORD from what each man acquired: gold articles — armlets, bracelets, signet rings, earrings, and pendants — to make atonement for ourselves before the LORD."
31.51: Moses and Eleazar the priest received the gold from them — all the worked articles.
31.52: The total gold of the offering presented to the LORD by the commanders of thousands and hundreds came to sixteen thousand seven hundred fifty shekels.
31.53: The rank-and-file soldiers had each taken spoil for themselves.
31.54: Moses and Eleazar the priest received the gold from the commanders and brought it into the tent of meeting as a memorial for the Israelites before the LORD.

32.1: The Reubenites and Gadites owned very large herds. When they saw that the lands of Jazer and Gilead were good pastureland,
32.2: the Gadites and Reubenites came to Moses, Eleazar the priest, and the community leaders, saying,
32.3: "Ataroth, Dibon, Jazer, Nimrah, Heshbon, Elealeh, Sebam, Nebo, and Beon —"
32.4: "— the land the LORD struck down before the Israelite congregation — is livestock country, and your servants have livestock."
32.5: "If we have found favor with you, please give this land to us as our possession. Do not make us cross the Jordan."
32.6: Moses replied to the Gadites and Reubenites, "Are your brothers to go off to war while you settle here?"
32.7: "Why would you discourage the Israelites from crossing into the land the LORD has given them?"
32.8: "Your ancestors did the same thing when I sent them from Kadesh-barnea to scout the land."
32.9: "They went up to the Valley of Eshcol, surveyed the land, then turned the heart of the Israelites away from entering the land the LORD had given them."
32.10: "The LORD's anger blazed that day and he swore:"
32.11: "'Not one of the men who came out of Egypt, twenty years old or above, will ever see the land I swore to give Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob — because they did not follow me wholeheartedly.'"
32.12: "'Only Caleb son of Jephunneh the Kenizzite and Joshua son of Nun were exceptions — they followed the LORD completely.'"
32.13: "The LORD's anger burned against Israel and he made them wander in the wilderness forty years, until every person who had sinned against him was gone."
32.14: "And here you are — sons of sinful men — risen in your fathers' footsteps, ready to stoke the LORD's burning anger against Israel still further."
32.15: "If you turn away from following him, he will again abandon them in the wilderness, and you will have destroyed all this people."
32.16: They came forward and said, "We will build sheepfolds here for our livestock and towns for our children,"
32.17: "— but we ourselves will arm for battle and march ahead of the Israelites until every tribe is settled. Our children will remain in the fortified towns, safe from the local population."
32.18: "We will not return home until every Israelite has received his inheritance."
32.19: "We will not take any inheritance with them across the Jordan, because our inheritance has come to us here on the east side."
32.20: Moses replied, "If you will truly do this — if you will arm yourselves for battle before the LORD,"
32.21: "and all your armed men cross the Jordan before the LORD until he has expelled his enemies before him,"
32.22: "— and when the land is subdued before the LORD, you may return. You will be free of obligation before the LORD and Israel, and this land will be yours."
32.23: "But if you do not do this, you have sinned against the LORD, and know that your sin will find you out."
32.24: "Go ahead — build towns for your children and pens for your flocks, and carry out what you have promised."
32.25: The Gadites and Reubenites said to Moses, "Your servants will do as my lord commands."
32.26: "Our children, wives, flocks, and all our livestock will remain in the cities of Gilead;"
32.27: "but we your servants will cross over, every one of us armed for war, before the LORD to battle, as my lord says."
32.28: Moses then gave instructions about them to Eleazar the priest, Joshua son of Nun, and the tribal leaders of the Israelites.
32.29: Moses said: "If the Gadites and Reubenites cross the Jordan armed before the LORD and the land is subdued, give them Gilead as their possession."
32.30: "But if they do not cross over armed, they will receive their share in Canaan like everyone else."
32.31: The Gadites and Reubenites replied, "What the LORD has said, we will do."
32.32: "We will cross over armed before the LORD into Canaan, and our inherited possession will remain here on the east side of the Jordan."
32.33: Moses assigned to the Gadites, the Reubenites, and the half-tribe of Manasseh son of Joseph the kingdom of Sihon king of the Amorites and the kingdom of Og king of Bashan — the land with its cities and surrounding territories.
32.34: The Gadites rebuilt Dibon, Ataroth, and Aroer,
32.36: Beth-nimrah and Beth-haran — all fortified cities — along with sheepfolds.
32.37: The Reubenites rebuilt Heshbon, Elealeh, and Kiriathaim,
32.38: Nebo and Baal-meon — renaming them — and Sibmah; they gave the rebuilt cities new names.
32.39: The sons of Machir son of Manasseh went to Gilead, captured it, and drove out the Amorites who lived there.
32.40: Moses assigned Gilead to Machir son of Manasseh, and he settled there.
32.41: Jair son of Manasseh went and took the surrounding villages, which he named Havvoth-jair.
32.42: Nobah went and captured Kenath and its surrounding villages, naming the place Nobah after himself.

33.1: These are the stages of the Israelites' journey out of Egypt, organized under the command of Moses and Aaron.
33.2: By the LORD's command, Moses recorded their departures stage by stage. These are the stages listed by their starting points:
33.3: They set out from Rameses in the first month, on the fifteenth day — the day after the Passover — in full sight of all the Egyptians, going out boldly.
33.4: while the Egyptians were burying their firstborn, whom the LORD had struck down; the LORD had also executed judgment on their gods.
33.5: The Israelites moved from Rameses and camped at Succoth.
33.6: From Succoth they moved to Etham, at the edge of the wilderness.
33.7: From Etham they turned back toward Pi-hahiroth, opposite Baal-zephon, and camped before Migdol.
33.8: From Hahiroth they passed through the middle of the sea into the wilderness, traveled three days through the wilderness of Etham, and camped at Marah.
33.9: From Marah they came to Elim, where there were twelve springs and seventy palms, and they camped there.
33.10: From Elim they camped by the Red Sea.
33.11: From the Red Sea they moved to the wilderness of Sin.
33.12: From the wilderness of Sin they camped at Dophkah.
33.13: From Dophkah they camped at Alush.
33.14: From Alush they camped at Rephidim — and there was no water for the people to drink.
33.15: From Rephidim they camped in the wilderness of Sinai.
33.16: From the wilderness of Sinai they camped at Kibroth-hattaavah.
33.17: From Kibroth-hattaavah they camped at Hazeroth.
33.18: From Hazeroth they camped at Rithmah.
33.19: From Rithmah they camped at Rimmon-perez.
33.20: From Rimmon-perez they camped at Libnah.
33.21: From Libnah they camped at Rissah.
33.22: From Rissah they camped at Kehelathah.
33.23: From Kehelathah they camped at Mount Shepher.
33.24: From Mount Shepher they camped at Haradah.
33.25: From Haradah they camped at Makheloth.
33.26: From Makheloth they camped at Tahath.
33.27: From Tahath they camped at Terah.
33.28: From Terah they camped at Mithkah.
33.29: From Mithkah they camped at Hashmonah.
33.30: From Hashmonah they camped at Moseroth.
33.31: From Moseroth they camped at Bene-jaakan.
33.32: From Bene-jaakan they camped at Hor-haggidgad.
33.33: From Hor-haggidgad they camped at Jotbathah.
33.34: From Jotbathah they camped at Abronah.
33.35: From Abronah they camped at Ezion-geber.
33.36: From Ezion-geber they camped in the wilderness of Zin — that is, Kadesh.
33.37: From Kadesh they camped at Mount Hor, on the border of Edom.
33.38: Aaron the priest went up Mount Hor at the LORD's command and died there in the fortieth year after the Israelites left Egypt, on the first day of the fifth month.
33.39: Aaron was one hundred twenty-three years old when he died at Mount Hor.
33.40: The Canaanite king of Arad, who lived in the Negeb in Canaan, heard that the Israelites were approaching.
33.41: From Mount Hor they camped at Zalmonah.
33.42: From Zalmonah they camped at Punon.
33.43: From Punon they camped at Oboth.
33.44: From Oboth they camped at Iye-abarim, on Moab's border.
33.45: From Iyim they camped at Dibon-gad.
33.46: From Dibon-gad they camped at Almon-diblathaim.
33.47: From Almon-diblathaim they camped in the Abarim mountains, before Nebo.
33.48: From the Abarim mountains they camped in the plains of Moab by the Jordan at Jericho.
33.49: Their camp stretched along the Jordan from Beth-jeshimoth to Abel-shittim in the plains of Moab.
33.50: The LORD spoke to Moses in the plains of Moab by the Jordan at Jericho, saying,
33.51: "Tell the Israelites: When you cross the Jordan into Canaan,"
33.52: "drive out all the land's inhabitants, smash all their carved stones, destroy all their cast metal images, and tear down all their high places."
33.53: "Take possession of the land and live in it, for I have given it to you."
33.54: "Distribute the land by lot among your clans — larger groups receive larger allotments, smaller groups smaller ones. The lot decides each person's place; inherit according to your ancestral tribes."
33.55: "But if you do not drive out the land's inhabitants, those you leave will be thorns in your eyes and splinters in your sides — they will harass you in the land where you live."
33.56: "And what I had planned to do to them, I will do to you."

34.1: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
34.2: "Command the Israelites: When you enter the land of Canaan, this is the land allotted to you as your inheritance — the land of Canaan within these boundaries:"
34.3: "Your southern boundary runs from the wilderness of Zin along Edom's flank; the southeastern corner begins at the far end of the Dead Sea."
34.4: "From the eastern corner it curves up the Scorpion Ascent, crosses to Zin, runs south of Kadesh-barnea, then continues to Hazar-addar and on to Azmon."
34.5: "From Azmon the border turns to the Wadi of Egypt and ends at the Mediterranean Sea."
34.6: "Your western border is the Mediterranean — the Great Sea itself."
34.7: "Your northern border: from the Great Sea, draw it to Mount Hor;"
34.8: "from Mount Hor extend it to the entrance of Hamath; the border ends at Zedad;"
34.9: "from there it continues to Ziphron and ends at Hazar-enan. That is the northern border."
34.10: "For the eastern border, mark it from Hazar-enan to Shepham;"
34.11: "the border descends from Shepham to Riblah, east of Ain, continues down to the eastern slope of the Sea of Kinnereth;"
34.12: "then it follows the Jordan down to the Dead Sea. That is your land, bounded on all sides."
34.13: Moses told the Israelites: "This is the land you will receive by lot — what the LORD has commanded to give to the nine and a half tribes."
34.14: "The tribe of Reuben, the tribe of Gad, and the half-tribe of Manasseh have already received their inheritance."
34.15: "Those two and a half tribes have received their portions on the east side of the Jordan, at Jericho, facing the sunrise."
34.16: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
34.17: "These are the men who will divide the land as your inheritance: Eleazar the priest and Joshua son of Nun."
34.18: "Appoint one leader from each tribe to assist in dividing the land."
34.19: "The names are: for Judah — Caleb son of Jephunneh;"
34.28: "and for Naphtali — Pedahel son of Ammihud."
34.29: These are the men the LORD commissioned to divide the inheritance among the Israelites in Canaan.

35.1: The LORD spoke to Moses in the plains of Moab by the Jordan at Jericho, saying,
35.2: "Command the Israelites to give the Levites, from their inherited land, towns to live in along with surrounding pasturelands."
35.3: "The towns will be for the Levites to live in; the surrounding pasturelands will be for their livestock and animals."
35.4: "The pasturelands given to the Levitical towns must extend a thousand cubits from the town wall outward in every direction."
35.5: "Measure two thousand cubits on each side — east, south, west, and north — with the town at the center. That defines the pastureland for the Levitical towns."
35.6: "Among the towns you give the Levites, include six cities of refuge where a person who kills accidentally may flee. Add forty-two other towns to these."
35.7: "The Levites' total is forty-eight towns with their surrounding pasturelands."
35.8: "When taking towns from Israel's holdings, take more from larger tribes and fewer from smaller ones — each tribe gives in proportion to its inherited land."
35.9: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
35.10: "Tell the Israelites: When you cross the Jordan into Canaan,"
35.11: "designate cities of refuge where a person who kills someone accidentally may escape."
35.12: "These cities will protect the manslayer from the blood avenger until the case is judged by the community."
35.13: "You shall designate six cities of refuge."
35.14: "Place three on the east side of the Jordan and three in Canaan proper."
35.15: "These six cities of refuge are open to Israelites, resident aliens, and foreigners among you — for anyone who kills unintentionally."
35.16: "If someone strikes another with an iron implement and causes death, that person is a murderer who must be executed."
35.17: "If someone strikes another with a stone large enough to kill and the person dies, that is murder — the murderer must be executed."
35.18: "If someone strikes another with a wooden weapon capable of causing death and the person dies, that is murder — the murderer must be executed."
35.19: "The blood avenger shall execute the murderer — when he encounters him, he shall put him to death."
35.20: "But if someone pushed another out of hatred, or hurled something at them while lying in ambush, and the person died,"
35.21: "or struck them with the fist out of enmity and caused death — that person is a murderer who must be executed. The blood avenger shall kill the murderer when they meet."
35.22: "But if someone shoved another suddenly, without hostility, or accidentally threw something at them without lying in ambush,"
35.23: "or accidentally dropped a lethal stone on someone — without them being enemies and without any malicious intent —"
35.24: "then the community must judge between the killer and the blood avenger according to these regulations."
35.25: "The congregation must protect the manslayer from the blood avenger and return him to his city of refuge. He must remain there until the death of the high priest who was anointed with the sacred oil."
35.26: "But if the manslayer ever ventures outside the boundary of the city of refuge to which he fled,"
35.27: "and the blood avenger finds him there and kills him, the avenger bears no guilt."
35.28: "The manslayer must stay in the city of refuge until the high priest dies; only then may he return to his own land."
35.29: "These regulations shall be a permanent law throughout your generations wherever you live."
35.30: "Anyone who kills must be executed on the testimony of witnesses; but no single witness alone may testify to put a person to death."
35.31: "You must not accept a ransom payment instead of the death of a murderer who deserves to die — he must be executed."
35.32: "Nor may you accept payment that would allow a manslayer to leave his city of refuge and resettle in the land before the high priest's death."
35.33: "Do not defile the land where you live; bloodshed defiles the land, and there is no atonement for it except through the blood of the one who shed it."
35.34: "Do not defile the land in which you live — the land where I myself dwell; for I, the LORD, dwell among the Israelites."

36.1: The heads of the clans of Gilead son of Machir, son of Manasseh, of the families of Joseph's descendants, came forward and addressed Moses and the tribal leaders, the heads of the Israelite clans.
36.2: They said: "The LORD commanded that the land be distributed by lot to the Israelites. Our lord was also commanded by the LORD to give our kinsman Zelophehad's inheritance to his daughters."
36.3: "But if those daughters marry into another tribe of Israel, their inheritance will pass out of our tribal portion and into the tribe they marry into — our territory shrinks permanently."
36.4: "And when the Jubilee comes, their inheritance will stay with the tribe they married into — our original allotment is permanently reduced."
36.5: At the LORD's direction Moses told the Israelites: "What the tribe of Joseph's descendants says is correct."
36.6: "Here is the LORD's ruling on Zelophehad's daughters: they may marry anyone they wish, but only within their father's tribal clan."
36.7: "No inheritance is to pass from one tribe to another; every Israelite is to keep the ancestral tribal inheritance intact."
36.8: "Every daughter in Israel who inherits land must marry within her father's tribal clan, so that every Israelite retains the inheritance of their ancestors."
36.9: "No tribal inheritance may move from one tribe to another; every tribe of Israel shall keep its own allotment."
36.10: The daughters of Zelophehad did exactly as the LORD commanded through Moses.
36.11: Mahlah, Tirzah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Noah — the daughters of Zelophehad — married their cousins, the sons of their uncles.
36.12: They married into the clans of Manasseh son of Joseph, and their inheritance remained within their father's tribal clan.
36.13: These are the commandments and ordinances that the LORD gave through Moses to the Israelites on the plains of Moab by the Jordan at Jericho.

19.1: The LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron, saying,
19.2: This is the statute of the law that the LORD has commanded: Speak to the Israelites and have them bring you a red heifer without defect, one with no blemish on which no yoke has ever been placed.
