19.3: You shall give her to Eleazar the priest, who shall take her outside the camp, where she is to be slaughtered before him.
19.4: Eleazar the priest shall take some of her blood with his finger and sprinkle it seven times toward the front of the tent of meeting.
19.5: Then the heifer is to be burned in his sight — her hide, her flesh, her blood, and her dung.
19.6: The priest shall then take cedar wood, hyssop, and scarlet yarn and throw them into the fire where the heifer is burning.
19.7: The priest must wash his clothes and bathe himself in water; after that he may return to the camp, but he will be unclean until evening.
19.8: The man who burned the heifer must also wash his clothes and bathe in water; he too will be unclean until evening.
19.9: A man who is clean shall gather up the heifer's ashes and store them outside the camp in a clean place; they are to be held in reserve for the whole Israelite community to be used in the purification water. It is a purification offering.
19.10: The one who gathers the ashes must wash his clothes and will be unclean until evening. This is a permanent statute for the Israelites and for any foreigner living among them.
19.11: Anyone who touches a human corpse will be unclean for seven days.
19.12: He is to cleanse himself with the purification water on the third day and on the seventh day, and then he will be clean; but if he fails to cleanse himself on the third day, he will not be clean on the seventh.
19.13: Anyone who touches a corpse and does not purify himself defiles the LORD's tabernacle; that person shall be cut off from Israel. Because the purification water was not sprinkled on him, he remains unclean — his uncleanness still clings to him.
19.14: This is the ruling when someone dies in a tent: all who enter the tent and everything in it will be unclean for seven days.
19.15: Every open container with no lid fastened on it will be unclean.
19.16: Anyone who touches in the open field a person killed by a sword, or a corpse, or a human bone, or a grave, will be unclean for seven days.
19.17: For an unclean person, some of the ashes of the burnt purification offering shall be taken and fresh water added to them in a vessel.
19.18: A clean person shall take hyssop, dip it in the water, and sprinkle it on the tent, on all the vessels, on all the people who were there, and on anyone who touched a bone, a person killed in battle, a corpse, or a grave.
19.19: The clean person shall sprinkle the unclean person on the third day and on the seventh day; on the seventh day the unclean person shall purify himself, wash his clothes, and bathe in water, and will be clean at evening.
19.20: But the person who is unclean and does not purify himself shall be cut off from the congregation; he has defiled the LORD's sanctuary. The purification water was not sprinkled on him; he is unclean.
19.21: This is a permanent statute: the one who sprinkles the purification water must wash his clothes, and whoever touches the purification water will be unclean until evening.
19.22: Whatever the unclean person touches will be unclean, and anyone who touches it will be unclean until evening.

20.1: In the first month the whole Israelite community came to the Desert of Zin and settled at Kadesh. There Miriam died and was buried.
20.2: There was no water for the community, and they assembled against Moses and Aaron.
20.3: The people quarreled with Moses, saying, 'If only we had perished when our brothers died before the LORD!'
20.4: 'Why did you bring the LORD's congregation into this wilderness for us and our livestock to die here?'
20.5: 'Why did you lead us up from Egypt to this wretched place? There is no grain, no figs, no vines, no pomegranates, and no water to drink.'
20.6: Moses and Aaron went from the assembly to the entrance of the tent of meeting and fell facedown, and the glory of the LORD appeared to them.
20.7: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
20.8: 'Take the staff, and assemble the community — you and your brother Aaron. Speak to the rock before their eyes, and it will yield its water; you will bring water out of the rock for the community and their livestock to drink.'
20.9: So Moses took the staff from before the LORD, as he had commanded.
20.10: Moses and Aaron gathered the assembly in front of the rock. Moses said to them, 'Listen, you rebels! Must we bring water out of this rock for you?'
20.11: Then Moses raised his hand and struck the rock twice with his staff. Water poured out abundantly, and the congregation and their livestock drank.
20.12: But the LORD said to Moses and Aaron, 'Because you did not trust me enough to uphold my holiness before the Israelites, you will not lead this congregation into the land I am giving them.'
20.13: These are the waters of Meribah, where the Israelites quarreled with the LORD and he showed himself holy among them.
20.14: Moses sent messengers from Kadesh to the king of Edom: 'Your brother Israel says this: You know all the hardship we have experienced —'
20.15: 'how our ancestors went down to Egypt, and we lived there a long time. The Egyptians oppressed us and our ancestors,'
20.16: 'but when we cried to the LORD, he heard our voice and sent a messenger who brought us out of Egypt. Now we are at Kadesh, a town on the edge of your territory.'
20.17: 'Please let us pass through your country. We will not go through fields or vineyards or drink well water; we will travel the King's Highway and not turn aside to the right or left until we have crossed your territory.'
20.18: But Edom answered, 'You may not pass through. If you try, we will come against you with the sword.'
20.19: The Israelites replied, 'We will go by the main road, and if we or our livestock drink any of your water, we will pay for it. We are asking only for the right of passage.'
20.20: But Edom said, 'You shall not pass,' and came out against them with a large and powerful army.
20.21: Since Edom refused to let Israel pass through its territory, Israel turned away from them.
20.22: The whole Israelite community set out from Kadesh and came to Mount Hor.
20.23: At Mount Hor, at the border of Edom, the LORD said to Moses and Aaron,
20.24: 'Aaron is about to be gathered to his people. He will not enter the land I am giving the Israelites, because both of you rebelled against my command at the waters of Meribah.'
20.25: 'Take Aaron and his son Eleazar and bring them up Mount Hor.'
20.26: 'Remove Aaron's priestly vestments and put them on his son Eleazar. Aaron will be gathered to his people — he will die there.'
20.27: Moses did as the LORD commanded. They climbed Mount Hor in the sight of the whole community.
20.28: Moses removed Aaron's vestments and put them on his son Eleazar. Aaron died there on the mountaintop, and Moses and Eleazar descended.
20.29: When the whole community saw that Aaron had died, all the house of Israel mourned for him thirty days.

21.1: When the Canaanite king of Arad, who lived in the Negev, heard that Israel was coming along the road to Atharim, he attacked Israel and captured some of them.
21.2: Israel made a vow to the LORD: 'If you will deliver this people into our hands, we will completely destroy their cities.'
21.3: The LORD listened to Israel's plea and gave the Canaanites into their hands. They completely destroyed them and their towns, and the place was named Hormah.
21.4: They traveled from Mount Hor along the road to the Red Sea to go around Edom, but the people grew impatient along the way.
21.5: The people spoke against God and against Moses: 'Why have you brought us up out of Egypt to die in the wilderness? There is no bread! There is no water! And we detest this miserable food!'
21.6: Then the LORD sent venomous snakes among them; they bit the people, and many Israelites died.
21.7: The people came to Moses and said, 'We have sinned by speaking against the LORD and against you. Pray that the LORD will take the snakes away.' So Moses prayed for the people.
21.8: The LORD said to Moses, 'Make a venomous snake and mount it on a pole; anyone who is bitten and looks at it will live.'
21.9: So Moses made a bronze snake and mounted it on a pole. When anyone who had been bitten looked at the bronze snake, they lived.
21.10: The Israelites moved on and camped at Oboth.
21.11: Setting out from Oboth, they camped at Iye-abarim in the wilderness east of Moab.
21.12: From there they set out and camped in the valley of Zered.
21.13: Setting out from there, they camped on the other side of the Arnon, which flows through the wilderness from the Amorite border; for the Arnon is the boundary between Moab and the Amorites.
21.14: That is why the Book of the Wars of the LORD says: '...Waheb in Suphah and the wadis — the Arnon'
21.15: 'and the slope of the wadis that leads to the settlement of Ar, resting on the border of Moab.'
21.16: From there they went to Beer, the well where the LORD said to Moses, 'Assemble the people, and I will give them water.'
21.17: Then Israel sang this song: 'Spring up, O well — sing to it!'
21.18: 'A well dug by princes, sunk by the nobles of the people, with the scepter, with their own staffs.' From the wilderness they went on to Mattanah,
21.19: from Mattanah to Nahaliel, from Nahaliel to Bamoth,
21.20: and from Bamoth to the valley in Moab, where the peak of Pisgah overlooks the wasteland.
21.21: Israel sent messengers to Sihon king of the Amorites, saying,
21.22: 'Let us pass through your land. We will not turn into fields or vineyards or drink well water; we will go along the King's Highway until we have crossed your territory.'
21.23: But Sihon would not let Israel pass. He mustered all his people and marched out into the wilderness against Israel; he came to Jahaz and fought Israel.
21.24: Israel defeated him with the sword and took possession of his land from the Arnon to the Jabbok, up to the Ammonite border, which was well fortified.
21.25: Israel captured all these towns and settled in all the Amorite towns, including Heshbon and all its surrounding settlements.
21.26: Heshbon was the royal city of Sihon king of the Amorites, who had fought against the previous king of Moab and taken all his land as far as the Arnon.
21.27: That is why the poets say: 'Come to Heshbon! Let it be rebuilt, let the city of Sihon be restored!'
21.28: 'For fire came out from Heshbon, a flame from Sihon's city; it devoured Ar of Moab and swallowed the heights of the Arnon.'
21.29: 'Woe to you, Moab! You are ruined, O people of Chemosh! He gave his sons as fugitives and his daughters as captives to Sihon king of the Amorites.'
21.30: 'But we have struck them down — Heshbon is ruined as far as Dibon; we have laid waste as far as Nophah, which reaches to Medeba.'
21.31: So Israel settled in the land of the Amorites.
21.32: Moses sent scouts to Jazer, and they captured its surrounding settlements and drove out the Amorites who were there.
21.33: Then they turned and went up the road to Bashan, and Og king of Bashan came out against them with all his people to fight at Edrei.
21.34: The LORD said to Moses, 'Do not fear him, for I have delivered him into your hand — him, his people, and his land. Do to him what you did to Sihon king of the Amorites who lived at Heshbon.'
21.35: So they struck him down along with his sons and his whole army, leaving no survivors, and took possession of his land.

22.1: The Israelites traveled on and camped in the plains of Moab across the Jordan from Jericho.
22.2: Balak son of Zippor saw all that Israel had done to the Amorites.
22.3: Moab was terrified of the people because they were so numerous, and Moab was filled with dread because of the Israelites.
22.4: Moab said to the elders of Midian, 'This horde will lick up everything around us the way an ox licks up the grass of the field.' Balak son of Zippor was king of Moab at that time.
22.5: He sent messengers to Balaam son of Beor at Pethor, near the Euphrates in the land of his people, with this message: 'A people has come out of Egypt; they cover the face of the land and are camped right next to me.'
22.6: 'Please come and curse these people for me, because they are too powerful for me. Perhaps then I can defeat them and drive them out of the land. For I know that whoever you bless is blessed, and whoever you curse is cursed.'
22.7: The elders of Moab and Midian left with divination fees in hand, came to Balaam, and delivered Balak's message.
22.8: He said to them, 'Spend the night here, and I will report back to you whatever the LORD tells me.' So the Moabite princes stayed with Balaam.
22.9: God came to Balaam and asked, 'Who are these men with you?'
22.10: Balaam told God, 'Balak son of Zippor, king of Moab, sent them with this message:'
22.11: 'A people has come out of Egypt and covers the face of the land. Come and curse them for me — perhaps I will then be able to fight them and drive them away.'
22.12: God said to Balaam, 'Do not go with them. Do not curse the people, because they are blessed.'
22.13: Balaam got up in the morning and said to Balak's princes, 'Go back to your own land; the LORD has refused to let me come with you.'
22.14: The Moabite princes returned to Balak and said, 'Balaam refused to come with us.'
22.15: Balak sent other princes, more numerous and more distinguished than the first.
22.16: They came to Balaam and said, 'This is what Balak son of Zippor says: Please do not let anything keep you from coming to me.'
22.17: 'I will reward you very handsomely and do whatever you say. Please come and curse these people for me.'
22.18: But Balaam replied to Balak's servants, 'Even if Balak gave me his palace full of silver and gold, I could not go beyond the command of the LORD my God, to do anything less or more.'
22.19: 'Nevertheless, stay here tonight as well, and I will find out what more the LORD says to me.'
22.20: That night God came to Balaam and said, 'Since these men have come to call you, go with them — but do only what I tell you.'
22.21: Balaam got up in the morning, saddled his donkey, and went with the Moabite princes.
22.22: But God was angry that he went, and the angel of the LORD stood in the road to oppose him. Balaam was riding his donkey, and his two servants were with him.
22.23: The donkey saw the angel of the LORD standing in the road with his sword drawn, so she turned off the road and went into a field. Balaam beat her to get her back on the road.
22.24: Then the angel of the LORD moved to a narrow path between two vineyards, with a wall on each side.
22.25: When the donkey saw the angel of the LORD, she pressed herself against the wall, crushing Balaam's foot against it, and he beat her again.
22.26: The angel of the LORD moved on ahead and stood in a narrow place where there was no room to turn either right or left.
22.27: When the donkey saw the angel of the LORD, she lay down under Balaam. He was furious and beat her with his staff.
22.28: Then the LORD opened the donkey's mouth, and she said to Balaam, 'What have I done to you that you have beaten me these three times?'
22.29: Balaam answered the donkey, 'You have made a fool of me! If only I had a sword in my hand — I would kill you right now!'
22.30: The donkey replied, 'Am I not your own donkey, which you have always ridden? Have I ever done this to you before?' 'No,' he admitted.
22.31: Then the LORD opened Balaam's eyes, and he saw the angel of the LORD standing in the road with his sword drawn. He bowed low and fell facedown.
22.32: The angel of the LORD said to him, 'Why have you beaten your donkey three times? I came here to oppose you, because your path is reckless before me.'
22.33: 'The donkey saw me and turned away from me three times. If she had not turned away, I would certainly have killed you by now, though I would have spared her.'
22.34: Balaam said to the angel of the LORD, 'I have sinned. I did not know you were standing in the road to oppose me. If you are displeased, I will go back.'
22.35: The angel of the LORD told him, 'Go with the men, but speak only what I tell you.' So Balaam went on with Balak's princes.
22.36: When Balak heard that Balaam was coming, he went out to meet him at the Moabite city on the Arnon, at the farthest point of the border.
22.37: Balak said to Balaam, 'Did I not send urgently to call you? Why didn't you come right away? Am I not able to honor you richly?'
22.38: Balaam replied, 'Well, I am here now. But understand this: I have no power to say anything on my own. I will speak only what God puts in my mouth.'
22.39: Balaam went with Balak to Kiriath-huzoth.
22.40: Balak sacrificed cattle and sheep and sent portions to Balaam and the princes who were with him.
22.41: The next morning Balak took Balaam up to Bamoth-baal, where he could see a portion of the Israelites.

23.1: Balaam said to Balak, 'Build me seven altars here and prepare seven bulls and seven rams.'
23.2: Balak did as Balaam said, and they offered a bull and a ram on each altar.
23.3: 'Stand here by your burnt offering,' Balaam told Balak, 'while I go off. Perhaps the LORD will come to meet me, and whatever he reveals I will tell you.' And he went to a bare height.
23.4: God met with Balaam, who said, 'I have set up seven altars and offered a bull and a ram on each one.'
23.5: The LORD put a word in Balaam's mouth and said, 'Go back to Balak and give him this message.'
23.6: When Balaam returned, Balak was standing by his burnt offering with all the Moabite princes.
23.7: Balaam delivered his oracle: 'Balak has brought me from Aram, from the mountains of the east — the king of Moab saying, Come, curse Jacob for me; come, denounce Israel.'
23.8: 'But how can I curse whom God has not cursed? How can I denounce whom the LORD has not denounced?'
23.9: 'From the rocky peaks I see them, from the hills I gaze down: here is a people who live apart and do not count themselves among the nations.'
23.10: 'Who can count the dust of Jacob or number the multitudes of Israel? May I die the death of the upright, and may my end be like theirs!'
23.11: Balak said to Balaam, 'What have you done to me? I brought you to curse my enemies, but you have blessed them outright!'
23.12: Balaam answered, 'Must I not be careful to say exactly what the LORD puts in my mouth?'
23.13: Balak said to him, 'Come with me to another place where you can see them — only the outskirts of the camp, not the whole mass of them — and curse them for me from there.'
23.14: He took him to the field of Zophim on the top of Pisgah and built seven altars, offering a bull and a ram on each.
23.15: Balaam said to Balak, 'Stand here by your burnt offering while I meet with the LORD over there.'
23.16: The LORD met with Balaam and put words in his mouth, then said, 'Go back to Balak and say this.'
23.17: When Balaam came back, Balak was standing by his offering with the Moabite princes. 'What has the LORD said?' Balak asked.
23.18: Balaam took up his oracle: 'Arise, Balak, and listen! Hear me, son of Zippor!'
23.19: 'God is not a man who lies, nor a son of man who changes his mind. Does he speak and not act? Does he promise and not fulfill?'
23.20: 'I have received a command to bless; he has blessed, and I cannot reverse it.'
23.21: 'He has not seen iniquity in Jacob nor found trouble in Israel. The LORD their God is with them; the acclamation of a king is among them.'
23.22: 'God brought them out of Egypt with the power of a wild ox.'
23.23: 'There is no omen that works against Jacob, no divination that prevails against Israel. In due time it will be said of Jacob and Israel: See what God has done!'
23.24: 'The people rise like a lioness; they rouse themselves like a lion that does not rest until it devours its prey and drinks the blood of the slain.'
23.25: Balak said to Balaam, 'Neither curse them at all nor bless them at all!'
23.26: Balaam replied to Balak, 'Did I not tell you that I must do everything the LORD says?'
23.27: Then Balak said, 'Come, let me take you to another place. Maybe it will please God for you to curse them from there.'
23.28: Balak led Balaam to the top of Peor, which overlooks the wasteland.
23.29: Balaam said to Balak, 'Build me seven altars here and prepare seven bulls and seven rams.'
23.30: Balak did as Balaam directed and offered a bull and a ram on each altar.

24.1: When Balaam saw that it pleased the LORD to bless Israel, he did not resort to divination as before but turned to face the wilderness.
24.2: He looked out and saw Israel encamped tribe by tribe, and the Spirit of God came on him.
24.3: He delivered his oracle: 'The utterance of Balaam son of Beor, the utterance of the man whose eyes are clear —'
24.4: 'The utterance of one who hears God's words and sees a vision from the Almighty, who falls prostrate with eyes wide open:'
24.5: 'How beautiful are your tents, Jacob, your dwelling places, Israel!'
24.6: 'Like valleys stretching out, like gardens beside a river, like aloe trees planted by the LORD, like cedars beside the waters.'
24.7: 'Water will flow from his buckets, his seed will have abundant water; his king will be greater than Agag, and his kingdom will be exalted.'
24.8: 'God brought him out of Egypt with the strength of a wild ox. He will devour hostile nations, crush their bones, and shatter them with his arrows.'
24.9: 'He crouches and lies down like a lion or a lioness; who dares rouse him? Blessed is everyone who blesses you, and cursed is everyone who curses you.'
24.10: Balak was furious with Balaam. He struck his palms together and said, 'I called you to curse my enemies, but you have blessed them three times over!'
24.11: 'Now get back to where you came from! I intended to honor you greatly, but the LORD has kept that honor from you.'
24.12: Balaam answered Balak, 'Did I not tell your messengers from the very beginning:'
24.13: 'Even if Balak gave me his palace full of silver and gold, I could not go against the LORD's command to do anything good or bad of my own will — whatever the LORD says, that is what I speak.'
24.14: 'Now I am going back to my own people. But let me warn you what this people will do to your people in the days to come.'
24.15: He delivered his oracle: 'The utterance of Balaam son of Beor, the utterance of the man whose eyes are clear —'
24.16: 'The utterance of one who hears God's words and knows the knowledge of the Most High, who sees the vision of the Almighty, who falls prostrate with eyes wide open:'
24.17: 'I see him, but not now; I behold him, but not near. A Star will come out of Jacob, a Scepter will rise from Israel; he will crush the skulls of Moab and destroy all the sons of Sheth.'
24.18: 'Edom will be conquered; Seir, his enemy, will also be conquered. But Israel will act valiantly.'
24.19: 'A ruler will come out of Jacob and destroy the survivors of the city.'
24.20: Then Balaam saw Amalek and delivered this oracle: 'Amalek was first among the nations, but their end is destruction.'
24.21: He looked at the Kenites and said: 'Your dwelling place is secure; your nest is set in rock.'
24.22: 'Yet the Kenite will be destroyed when Asshur takes you captive.'
24.23: Then he spoke another oracle: 'Alas, who can survive when God brings this about?'
24.24: 'Ships will come from the coasts of Kittim; they will oppress Asshur and Eber, but they too will come to ruin.'
24.25: Then Balaam got up and returned home, and Balak went his way.

13.1: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
13.2: Send men to spy out the land of Canaan, which I am giving to the Israelites. From each of their ancestral tribes send one man — every one a leader among them.
13.3: So Moses sent them from the wilderness of Paran at the LORD's command. All of them were leading men among the Israelites.
13.4: These were their names: from the tribe of Reuben, Shammua son of Zaccur.
13.5: From the tribe of Simeon, Shaphat son of Hori.
13.6: From the tribe of Judah, Caleb son of Jephunneh.
13.7: From the tribe of Issachar, Igal son of Joseph.
13.8: From the tribe of Ephraim, Hoshea son of Nun.
13.9: From the tribe of Benjamin, Palti son of Raphu.
13.10: From the tribe of Zebulun, Gaddiel son of Sodi.
13.11: From the tribe of Joseph — from the tribe of Manasseh — Gaddi son of Susi.
13.12: From the tribe of Dan, Ammiel son of Gemalli.
13.13: From the tribe of Asher, Sethur son of Michael.
13.14: From the tribe of Naphtali, Nahbi son of Vophsi.
13.15: From the tribe of Gad, Geuel son of Machi.
13.16: These are the names of the men Moses sent to spy out the land. Moses called Hoshea son of Nun, Joshua.
13.17: Moses sent them to spy out the land of Canaan and said to them: Go up through the Negeb and up into the hill country.
13.18: See what the land is like, and whether the people living there are strong or weak, whether they are few or many,
13.19: and whether the land they live in is good or bad, and whether the cities they inhabit are open camps or fortified strongholds,
13.20: and whether the land is fertile or lean, and whether there are trees in it or not. Be bold, and bring back some of the fruit of the land. Now it was the season of the first ripe grapes.
13.21: So they went up and spied out the land from the wilderness of Zin to Rehob, near Lebo-hamath.
13.22: They went up through the Negeb and came to Hebron. Ahiman, Sheshai, and Talmai, the descendants of Anak, were there. Hebron had been built seven years before Zoan in Egypt.
13.23: They came to the valley of Eshcol and cut down from there a branch with a single cluster of grapes, which they carried on a pole between two men; they also brought back pomegranates and figs.
13.24: That place was called the valley of Eshcol, because of the cluster the Israelites cut down there.
13.25: After forty days they returned from spying out the land.
13.26: They came to Moses and Aaron and to the whole congregation of Israel in the wilderness of Paran at Kadesh. They brought back word to them and to the whole congregation and showed them the fruit of the land.
13.27: They told Moses: We came to the land you sent us to, and it does indeed flow with milk and honey. Here is its fruit.
13.28: However, the people living in the land are strong and the cities are large and fortified. We also saw the descendants of Anak there.
13.29: The Amalekites dwell in the Negeb. The Hittites, Jebusites, and Amorites live in the hill country. The Canaanites dwell by the sea and along the Jordan.
13.30: But Caleb quieted the people before Moses and said, Let us go up at once and occupy it, for we are well able to overcome it.
13.31: But the men who had gone up with him said, We are not able to go up against the people, for they are stronger than we are.
13.32: So they brought a bad report of the land to the Israelites, saying: The land we traveled through to spy out is a land that devours its inhabitants, and all the people we saw there are of great stature.
13.33: We saw the Nephilim there — the descendants of Anak, who come from the Nephilim. We seemed to ourselves like grasshoppers, and so we seemed to them.

14.1: Then all the congregation raised a loud cry, and the people wept that night.
14.2: All the Israelites grumbled against Moses and Aaron. The whole congregation said to them: If only we had died in the land of Egypt! Or if only we had died in this wilderness!
14.3: Why is the LORD bringing us into this land, to fall by the sword? Our wives and children will become prey. Would it not be better to go back to Egypt?
14.4: And they said to one another: Let us appoint a leader and return to Egypt.
14.5: Then Moses and Aaron fell facedown before the whole assembly of the congregation of Israel.
14.6: And Joshua son of Nun and Caleb son of Jephunneh, who were among those who had spied out the land, tore their clothes
14.7: and said to the whole congregation of Israel: The land we traveled through to spy out is an exceedingly good land.
14.8: If the LORD is pleased with us, he will bring us into this land and give it to us — a land that flows with milk and honey.
14.9: Only do not rebel against the LORD, and do not fear the people of the land, for they are bread for us. Their protection is removed from them, and the LORD is with us. Do not fear them.
14.10: But the whole congregation said to stone them with stones. Then the glory of the LORD appeared at the tent of meeting to all the Israelites.
14.11: The LORD said to Moses: How long will this people despise me? And how long will they not believe me, in spite of all the signs I have done among them?
14.12: I will strike them with pestilence and disinherit them, and I will make of you a nation greater and mightier than they.
14.13: But Moses said to the LORD: If you do this, the Egyptians will hear of it — for you brought this people up from among them by your great power —
14.14: and they will tell the inhabitants of this land. They have heard that you, LORD, are in the midst of this people, that you, LORD, are seen face to face, that your cloud stands over them, and that you go before them in a pillar of cloud by day and in a pillar of fire by night.
14.15: If you kill this people as one man, then the nations who have heard your fame will say,
14.16: The LORD was not able to bring this people into the land he swore to give to them, and so he has killed them in the wilderness.
14.17: And now, please let the power of the Lord be great as you have promised, saying,
14.18: The LORD is slow to anger and abounding in steadfast love, forgiving iniquity and transgression, but by no means clearing the guilty, visiting the iniquity of the fathers on the children to the third and fourth generation.
14.19: Please pardon the iniquity of this people according to the greatness of your steadfast love, just as you have forgiven this people from Egypt until now.
14.20: The LORD said: I have pardoned, according to your word.
14.21: But as I live, and as all the earth shall be filled with the glory of the LORD,
14.22: none of the men who have seen my glory and my signs that I did in Egypt and in the wilderness, and yet have tested me these ten times and not obeyed my voice,
14.23: shall see the land I swore to give to their fathers. None of those who despised me shall see it.
14.24: But my servant Caleb, because he has a different spirit and has followed me fully, I will bring into the land where he went, and his descendants shall possess it.
14.25: Since the Amalekites and the Canaanites dwell in the valleys, turn back tomorrow and set out for the wilderness by the way to the Red Sea.
14.26: The LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron, saying,
14.27: How long shall I bear with this wicked congregation that grumbles against me? I have heard the grumbling of the Israelites, which they grumble against me.
14.28: Say to them: As I live, declares the LORD, I will do to you just what you have said in my hearing:
14.29: your dead bodies shall fall in this wilderness, and of all your number who were registered in the census from twenty years old and upward, who have grumbled against me —
14.30: Not one of you shall come into the land where I swore to settle you — except Caleb son of Jephunneh and Joshua son of Nun.
14.31: But your little ones, who you said would become prey — I will bring them in, and they shall know the land you have rejected.
14.32: But as for you, your dead bodies shall fall in this wilderness.
14.33: Your children shall wander in the wilderness for forty years and shall bear the weight of your faithlessness, until the last of your dead bodies lies in the wilderness.
14.34: According to the number of days you spied out the land — forty days — a year for each day, you shall bear your iniquity for forty years, and you shall know the cost of opposing me.
14.35: I, the LORD, have spoken. Surely I will do this to all this wicked congregation gathered together against me: in this wilderness they shall come to a full end, and there they shall die.
14.36: The men Moses sent to spy out the land, who returned and made the whole congregation grumble against him by bringing back a bad report about the land —
14.37: those men who brought up a bad report about the land died by plague before the LORD.
14.38: Of those men who went to spy out the land, only Joshua son of Nun and Caleb son of Jephunneh remained alive.
14.39: Moses told these words to all the Israelites, and the people mourned greatly.
14.40: They rose early in the morning and went up toward the heights of the hill country, saying: Here we are. We will go up to the place the LORD promised, for we have sinned.
14.41: But Moses said: Why are you now transgressing the LORD's command? This will not succeed.
14.42: Do not go up, for the LORD is not among you, lest you be struck down before your enemies.
14.43: For the Amalekites and the Canaanites are there before you, and you shall fall by the sword. Because you have turned back from the LORD, the LORD will not be with you.
14.44: But they presumed to go up to the heights of the hill country, even though the ark of the covenant of the LORD and Moses had not left the camp.
14.45: Then the Amalekites and the Canaanites who lived in that hill country came down and defeated them, driving them back as far as Hormah.

15.1: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
15.2: Speak to the Israelites and say to them: When you come into the land you are to inhabit, which I am giving you,
15.3: and you make a food offering by fire to the LORD — a burnt offering, or a sacrifice to fulfill a vow, or a freewill offering, or at your appointed festivals — to produce a pleasing aroma to the LORD, from herd or flock,
15.4: the one presenting the offering shall bring a grain offering of one-tenth of an ephah of fine flour mixed with one-quarter of a hin of oil.
15.5: Also prepare a quarter of a hin of wine for a drink offering to accompany the burnt offering or sacrifice for each lamb.
15.6: For a ram, prepare a grain offering of two-tenths of an ephah of fine flour mixed with one-third of a hin of oil,
15.7: and for a drink offering offer one-third of a hin of wine, a pleasing aroma to the LORD.
15.8: When you prepare a young bull as a burnt offering, or a sacrifice to fulfill a vow, or as a fellowship offering to the LORD,
15.9: bring with the bull a grain offering of three-tenths of an ephah of fine flour mixed with half a hin of oil,
15.10: and for a drink offering bring half a hin of wine, a food offering, a pleasing aroma to the LORD.
15.11: This shall be done for each bull, each ram, and each lamb or goat.
15.12: Whatever the number of animals you offer, do this for each one according to their number.
15.13: Every native Israelite shall follow this procedure when presenting a food offering of pleasing aroma to the LORD.
15.14: And if a resident alien living among you, or anyone else with you throughout your generations, presents a food offering of pleasing aroma to the LORD, he shall do just as you do.
15.15: There shall be one statute for the congregation — for you and for the resident alien living with you — a permanent statute throughout your generations. Before the LORD, the resident alien shall be the same as you.
15.16: One law and one rule shall apply to you and to the resident alien living among you.
15.17: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
15.18: Speak to the Israelites and say to them: When you come into the land to which I am bringing you
15.19: and you eat from the produce of the land, you shall present a contribution as a gift to the LORD.
15.20: From the first of your dough you shall present a loaf as a contribution — as with the contribution from the threshing floor, you shall present it.
15.21: From the first of your dough you shall give the LORD a contribution throughout your generations.
15.22: But if you sin unintentionally and do not observe all these commandments that the LORD spoke to Moses —
15.23: all that the LORD commanded you through Moses, from the day the LORD gave the command and onward throughout your generations —
15.24: if it was done unintentionally, without the congregation's knowledge, then the whole congregation shall offer one young bull for a burnt offering as a pleasing aroma to the LORD, with its prescribed grain offering and drink offering, and one male goat for a sin offering.
15.25: The priest shall make atonement for the whole congregation of Israel, and they shall be forgiven, for it was unintentional. They shall bring their offering — a food offering to the LORD — and their sin offering before the LORD for their unintentional sin.
15.26: The whole congregation of Israel shall be forgiven, along with the resident alien living among them, since all the people acted unintentionally.
15.27: If only one person sins unintentionally, that person shall bring a yearling female goat for a sin offering.
15.28: The priest shall make atonement before the LORD for the person who sinned unintentionally, making atonement for that person, and he or she shall be forgiven.
15.29: You shall have one law for the person who acts unintentionally, whether native-born among the Israelites or a resident alien living among them.
15.30: But the person who acts defiantly, whether native-born or a resident alien — that person blasphemes the LORD and shall be cut off from among the people.
15.31: Because he has despised the word of the LORD and broken his commandment, that person shall be utterly cut off; his guilt is upon him.
15.32: While the Israelites were in the wilderness, they found a man gathering wood on the Sabbath day.
15.33: Those who found him gathering wood brought him to Moses and Aaron and to the whole congregation.
15.34: They put him in custody, because it had not been made clear what should be done to him.
15.35: The LORD said to Moses: The man shall be put to death; the whole congregation shall stone him with stones outside the camp.
15.36: So all the congregation brought him outside the camp and stoned him to death, as the LORD had commanded Moses.
15.37: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
15.38: Speak to the Israelites and tell them to make tassels on the corners of their garments throughout their generations, and to put a blue cord on the tassel of each corner.
15.39: The tassels shall be for you to look at, so that you may remember all the LORD's commandments and do them, and not follow your own heart and eyes, which lead you astray.
15.40: So you shall remember and do all my commandments, and you shall be holy to your God.
15.41: I am the LORD your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt to be your God. I am the LORD your God.

16.1: Now Korah son of Izhar son of Kohath son of Levi, and Dathan and Abiram sons of Eliab, and On son of Peleth — sons of Reuben — took action.
16.2: They rose up against Moses, together with 250 Israelites who were leaders of the congregation — men called to the assembly, men of repute.
16.3: They assembled against Moses and Aaron and said to them: You have gone too far! The whole congregation is holy, every one of them, and the LORD is in their midst. Why then do you exalt yourselves above the assembly of the LORD?
16.4: When Moses heard this, he fell facedown.
16.5: Then he spoke to Korah and all his company, saying: Tomorrow morning the LORD will show who is his and who is holy, and will bring that person near to himself. The one he chooses he will bring near.
16.6: Do this: take censers, Korah and all your company,
16.7: put fire in them and place incense on them before the LORD tomorrow. The man the LORD chooses shall be the holy one. It is you Levites who have gone too far.
16.8: Moses also said to Korah: Listen now, you sons of Levi.
16.9: Is it too small a thing for you that the God of Israel has separated you from the congregation of Israel to bring you near to himself — to do the service of the tabernacle of the LORD and to stand before the congregation to minister to them?
16.10: He has brought you near, and all your fellow Levites with you. And yet you seek the priesthood too?
16.11: Therefore it is against the LORD that you and all your company have gathered. For what is Aaron, that you grumble against him?
16.12: Moses summoned Dathan and Abiram sons of Eliab, but they said: We will not come up.
16.13: Is it too small a thing that you have brought us up out of a land flowing with milk and honey to kill us in the wilderness? Must you also make yourself a ruler over us?
16.14: You have not brought us into a land flowing with milk and honey, nor have you given us an inheritance of fields and vineyards. Do you think you can blind these men? We will not come up.
16.15: Then Moses was very angry and said to the LORD: Do not accept their offering. I have not taken one donkey from them, nor have I harmed one of them.
16.16: Moses said to Korah: Tomorrow you and all your company shall appear before the LORD — you and they and Aaron.
16.17: Each man shall take his censer, put incense in it, and bring his censer before the LORD — 250 censers in all, together with yours and Aaron's, each with his own.
16.18: So each man took his censer, put fire in it, placed incense on it, and stood at the entrance of the tent of meeting with Moses and Aaron.
16.19: Korah gathered the whole congregation against them at the entrance of the tent of meeting. And the glory of the LORD appeared to the whole congregation.
16.20: The LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron, saying,
16.21: Separate yourselves from this congregation so that I may consume them in an instant.
16.22: But they fell facedown and said: O God, the God of the spirits of all flesh, shall one man sin and you be angry with the whole congregation?
16.23: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
16.24: Tell the congregation to move away from the dwellings of Korah, Dathan, and Abiram.
16.25: Moses rose and went to Dathan and Abiram, and the elders of Israel followed him.
16.26: He spoke to the congregation, saying: Move away from the tents of these wicked men and touch nothing that belongs to them, lest you be swept away in all their sins.
16.27: So they moved away from the dwellings of Korah, Dathan, and Abiram on every side. And Dathan and Abiram came out and stood at the entrance of their tents, together with their wives, their sons, and their little children.
16.28: Moses said: By this you shall know that the LORD has sent me to do all these works, and that it has not been of my own accord:
16.29: If these men die the common death of all mankind, or if they are visited by the fate that comes to all, then the LORD has not sent me.
16.30: But if the LORD creates something entirely new — if the ground opens its mouth and swallows them with all that belongs to them, so that they go down alive into Sheol — then you will know that these men have despised the LORD.
16.31: As soon as he finished speaking all these words, the ground under them split apart.
16.32: The earth opened its mouth and swallowed them up, along with their households, all the people belonging to Korah, and all their possessions.
16.33: They went down alive into Sheol, they and all who belonged to them. The earth closed over them, and they perished from the midst of the congregation.
16.34: And all the Israelites around them fled at their cry, for they said: Lest the earth swallow us up also!
16.35: Fire also came out from the LORD and consumed the 250 men who were offering incense.
16.36: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
16.37: Tell Eleazar son of Aaron the priest to take up the censers from the burning, and scatter the coals at a distance, for the censers are holy.
16.38: The censers of these men who sinned at the cost of their own lives — have them hammered into flat sheets as a covering for the altar. For they were presented before the LORD and are holy. They shall be a sign to the Israelites.
16.39: So Eleazar the priest took the bronze censers with which the burned men had offered incense and had them hammered flat to cover the altar,
16.40: a reminder to the Israelites that no unauthorized person — not of Aaron's line — should come near to offer incense before the LORD, or he would end up like Korah and his company. This was as the LORD had directed through Moses.
16.41: But the next day the whole congregation of Israel grumbled against Moses and Aaron, saying: You have killed the people of the LORD.
16.42: When the congregation assembled against Moses and Aaron, they turned toward the tent of meeting — the cloud had covered it and the glory of the LORD appeared.
16.43: Moses and Aaron went to the front of the tent of meeting.
16.44: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
16.45: Get away from this congregation so that I may consume them in an instant. And they fell facedown.
16.46: Moses said to Aaron: Take your censer, put fire from the altar in it, place incense on it, and carry it quickly to the congregation and make atonement for them. For wrath has gone out from the LORD; the plague has begun.
16.47: Aaron took his censer as Moses had said, ran into the middle of the congregation — the plague had indeed begun among the people — placed incense on the censer, and made atonement for the people.
16.48: He stood between the dead and the living, and the plague was stopped.
16.49: Those who died in the plague were 14,700, besides those who had died in the matter of Korah.
16.50: Then Aaron returned to Moses at the entrance of the tent of meeting, for the plague had been stopped.

17.1: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
17.2: Speak to the Israelites and collect from them one staff per ancestral house — from all their leaders, one staff per ancestral house — twelve staffs in all. Write each man's name on his staff.
17.3: Write Aaron's name on Levi's staff, for there shall be one staff for each ancestral house.
17.4: Place them in the tent of meeting before the covenant document, where I meet with you.
17.5: The man I choose — his staff will sprout. In this way I will put an end to the Israelites' grumbling against you, which they keep grumbling against you.
17.6: Moses spoke to the Israelites, and each of their leaders gave him a staff — one staff per leader, twelve staffs by their ancestral houses. Aaron's staff was among them.
17.7: Moses placed the staffs before the LORD in the tent of the covenant.
17.8: The next day Moses went into the tent of the covenant, and there was Aaron's staff — representing the house of Levi — it had budded, put out buds, produced blossoms, and borne ripe almonds.
17.9: Moses brought out all the staffs from before the LORD to all the Israelites. They looked, and each man took his own staff.
17.10: The LORD said to Moses: Put Aaron's staff back before the covenant document, to be kept as a sign against the rebels, to put an end to their grumbling against me, so that they do not die.
17.11: Moses did so; he did just as the LORD commanded him.
17.12: The Israelites said to Moses: We are dying, we are perishing, we are all lost.
17.13: Anyone who comes near the tabernacle of the LORD shall die. Are we all going to die?

18.1: The LORD said to Aaron: You and your sons and your ancestral house with you shall bear the guilt of sins against the sanctuary, and you and your sons alone shall bear the guilt of sins against your priesthood.
18.2: Also bring with you your kinsmen from the tribe of Levi, your father's tribe, so that they may join you and assist you when you and your sons serve before the tent of the covenant.
18.3: They shall fulfill their duties to you and to the whole tent, but they shall not come near the furnishings of the sanctuary or the altar, lest both they and you die.
18.4: They shall join you and keep the charge of the tent of meeting — all the service of the tent — and no unauthorized person shall come near you.
18.5: You shall be responsible for the sanctuary and the altar, so that wrath does not fall on the Israelites again.
18.6: Behold, I myself have taken your kinsmen the Levites from among the Israelites. They are given to you as a gift for the LORD, to do the service of the tent of meeting.
18.7: But you and your sons shall attend to your priestly duties for everything at the altar and within the veil, and you shall serve. I give you your priesthood as a gift of service. Any unauthorized person who comes near shall be put to death.
18.8: The LORD said to Aaron: I also give you charge of the contributions made to me from all the holy gifts of the Israelites. I give them to you and your sons as your portion — a perpetual statute.
18.9: From the most holy portion — whatever is not burned up — every offering, every grain offering, every sin offering, and every guilt offering they bring to me as most holy shall belong to you and your sons.
18.10: In the most holy place you shall eat it; every male shall eat it. It is most holy for you.
18.11: This also is yours: the contribution of their gift, together with all the wave offerings of the Israelites. I give them to you and your sons and daughters as a permanent statute. Everyone in your household who is ceremonially clean may eat it.
18.12: All the best of the oil and all the best of the wine and grain — the firstfruits they give to the LORD — I give to you.
18.13: The first ripe produce of all that is in their land, which they bring to the LORD, shall be yours. Everyone in your household who is clean may eat it.
18.14: Everything in Israel that is devoted to destruction shall be yours.
18.15: Every firstborn of all flesh, whether human or animal, that they offer to the LORD shall be yours. Nevertheless, you shall redeem every firstborn human, and the firstborn of unclean animals you shall also redeem.
18.16: Their redemption price, from a month old, you shall fix at five shekels of silver by the sanctuary shekel — twenty gerahs.
18.17: But the firstborn of a cow, a sheep, or a goat you shall not redeem; they are holy. Sprinkle their blood on the altar and burn their fat as a food offering, a pleasing aroma to the LORD.
18.18: Their meat shall be yours — like the breast of the wave offering and the right thigh, it belongs to you.
18.19: All the contributions of the holy gifts that the Israelites offer to the LORD I give to you and your sons and daughters as a permanent statute. It is an everlasting covenant of salt before the LORD for you and your descendants.
18.20: The LORD said to Aaron: You shall have no inheritance in their land, nor shall you have any portion among them. I am your portion and your inheritance among the Israelites.
18.21: To the Levites I have given all the tithes in Israel as their inheritance in return for the service they perform — the service of the tent of meeting.
18.22: The Israelites must not come near the tent of meeting, lest they bear sin and die.
18.23: Only the Levites shall serve at the tent of meeting, and they shall bear responsibility for any offenses against it. This is a permanent statute throughout your generations: among the Israelites the Levites shall have no inheritance.
18.24: For the tithe of the Israelites, which they present as a contribution to the LORD, I have given to the Levites for an inheritance. Therefore I have said of them: Among the Israelites they shall have no inheritance.
18.25: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
18.26: Speak to the Levites and say to them: When you receive from the Israelites the tithe that I have given you as your inheritance, you shall present a contribution from it to the LORD — a tithe of the tithe.
18.27: Your contribution shall be credited to you as though it were the grain of the threshing floor and the produce of the winepress.
18.28: So you shall also present a contribution to the LORD from all your tithes that you receive from the Israelites, and from these you shall give the LORD's contribution to Aaron the priest.
18.29: Out of all the gifts you receive, you shall present every contribution owed to the LORD from the very best of it — the holy portion from it.
18.30: Say to them: When you have presented the best of it, the remainder shall be counted to the Levites as the produce of the threshing floor and the produce of the winepress.
18.31: You and your households may eat it anywhere, for it is your payment for your service at the tent of meeting.
18.32: You will bear no guilt because of it, once you have contributed the best of it. But do not profane the holy gifts of the Israelites, or you will die.

25.1: While Israel was dwelling in Shittim, the people began to commit fornication with the daughters of Moab.
25.2: The Moabite women invited the people to the sacrifices of their gods, and the people ate and bowed down to their gods.
25.3: Israel yoked itself to Baal of Peor, and the LORD's anger burned against Israel.
25.4: The LORD said to Moses: Take all the heads of the people and put them to death publicly before the LORD in the sight of the sun, so that the fierce anger of the LORD may turn from Israel.
25.5: Moses said to the judges of Israel: Each of you must put to death those among his men who have attached themselves to Baal of Peor.
25.6: Just then, right in front of Moses and the whole congregation — who were weeping at the entrance of the tent of meeting — an Israelite man came and brought a Midianite woman to his family.
25.7: When Phinehas son of Eleazar, son of Aaron the priest, saw it, he rose from among the congregation and took a spear in his hand.
25.8: and followed the man of Israel into the inner chamber and drove it through both of them — the man and the woman through her abdomen. So the plague stopped among the Israelites.
25.9: Those who died in the plague numbered twenty-four thousand.
25.10: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
25.11: Phinehas son of Eleazar son of Aaron the priest has turned my wrath away from the Israelites by being zealous for my cause among them, so that I did not consume the Israelites in my jealous anger.
25.12: Therefore say: I am giving him my covenant of peace.
25.13: It shall be for him and his descendants after him a covenant of perpetual priesthood, because he was zealous for his God and made atonement for the Israelites.
25.14: The Israelite who was killed — the one killed with the Midianite woman — was Zimri son of Salu, a leader of a father's house among the Simeonites.
25.15: The Midianite woman who was killed was Cozbi daughter of Zur; he was head of a people of a father's house in Midian.
25.16: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
25.17: Treat the Midianites as enemies and strike them down.
25.18: For they dealt treacherously with you through their schemes — in the Peor affair and in the matter of Cozbi, daughter of a Midianite leader, who was killed on the day of the plague in the matter of Peor.

26.1: After the plague, the LORD spoke to Moses and Eleazar son of Aaron the priest, saying,
26.2: Take a census of the whole congregation of Israel — all males twenty years old and upward, by their ancestral houses, who are able to go to war.
26.3: Moses and Eleazar the priest spoke with them in the plains of Moab beside the Jordan across from Jericho, saying,
26.4: Register everyone twenty years and older, as the LORD had commanded Moses — these being the Israelites who came out of Egypt.
26.5: Reuben, the firstborn of Israel. Reuben's clans: from Hanoch, the Hanochites; from Pallu, the Palluites;
26.6: from Hezron, the Hezronites; from Carmi, the Carmites.
26.7: These are the clans of the Reubenites. Those numbered: 43,730.
26.9: The sons of Eliab: Nemuel, Dathan, and Abiram. This is the Dathan and Abiram who were prominent in the congregation — those who challenged Moses and Aaron as part of Korah's faction when they rebelled against the LORD.
26.10: The earth opened its mouth and swallowed them along with Korah when that company died — when fire devoured the two hundred and fifty men. They became a warning sign.
26.11: But the sons of Korah did not die.
26.12: The clans of Simeon: from Nemuel, the Nemuelites; from Jamin, the Jaminites; from Jachin, the Jachinites;
26.13: from Zerah, the Zerahites; from Shaul, the Shaulites.
26.14: These are the clans of the Simeonites. Total: 22,200.
26.15: The clans of Gad: from Zephon, the Zephonites; from Haggi, the Haggites; from Shuni, the Shunites;
26.16: from Ozni, the Oznites; from Eri, the Erites;
26.17: from Arod, the Arodites; from Areli, the Arelites.
26.18: These are the clans of Gad according to those numbered: 40,500.
26.19: The sons of Judah: Er and Onan. But Er and Onan died in the land of Canaan.
26.20: The surviving clans of Judah: from Shelah, the Shelanites; from Pharez, the Pharzites; from Zerah, the Zerahites.
26.21: The sons of Pharez: from Hezron, the Hezronites; from Hamul, the Hamulites.
26.22: These are the clans of Judah according to those numbered: 76,500.
26.23: The clans of Issachar: from Tola, the Tolaites; from Pua, the Punites;
26.24: from Jashub, the Jashubites; from Shimron, the Shimronites.
26.25: These are the clans of Issachar according to those numbered: 64,300.
26.26: The clans of Zebulun: from Sered, the Sardites; from Elon, the Elonites; from Jahleel, the Jahleelites.
26.27: These are the clans of Zebulun according to those numbered: 60,500.
26.28: The sons of Joseph, by their clans: Manasseh and Ephraim.
26.29: From the sons of Manasseh: from Machir, the Machirites — Machir fathered Gilead — from Gilead, the Gileadites.
26.30: The sons of Gilead: from Jeezer, the Jeezerites; from Helek, the Helekites;
26.31: from Asriel, the Asrielites; from Shechem, the Shechemites;
26.32: from Shemida, the Shemidaites; from Hepher, the Hepherites.
26.33: Zelophehad son of Hepher had no sons, only daughters: Mahlah, Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Tirzah.
26.34: These are the clans of Manasseh. Those numbered: 52,700.
26.35: The clans of Ephraim: from Shuthelah, the Shuthalhites; from Becher, the Bachrites; from Tahan, the Tahanites.
26.36: The sons of Shuthelah: from Eran, the Eranites.
26.37: These are the clans of Ephraim according to those numbered: 32,500. These are the sons of Joseph by their clans.
26.38: The clans of Benjamin: from Bela, the Belaites; from Ashbel, the Ashbelites; from Ahiram, the Ahiramites;
26.39: from Shupham, the Shuphamites; from Hupham, the Huphamites.
26.40: The sons of Bela were Ard and Naaman: from Ard, the Ardites; from Naaman, the Naamites.
26.41: These are the sons of Benjamin according to their clans. Those numbered: 45,600.
26.42: The clans of Dan after their clans: from Shuham, the Shuhamites. These are the clans of Dan by their clans.
26.43: All the clans of the Shuhamites according to those numbered: 64,400.
26.44: The clans of Asher: from Jimna, the Jimnites; from Jesui, the Jesuites; from Beriah, the Beriites.
26.45: From the sons of Beriah: from Heber, the Heberites; from Malchiel, the Malchielites.
26.46: The name of Asher's daughter was Serah.
26.47: These are the clans of Asher according to those numbered: 53,400.
26.48: The clans of Naphtali: from Jahzeel, the Jahzeelites; from Guni, the Gunites;
26.49: from Jezer, the Jezerites; from Shillem, the Shillemites.
26.50: These are the clans of Naphtali according to their clans. Those numbered: 45,400.
26.51: These were the total numbered of the Israelites: 601,730.
26.52: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
26.53: To these the land shall be divided as an inheritance according to the number of names.
26.54: To a larger group give a larger inheritance, and to a smaller group a smaller one. Each shall receive its inheritance proportioned to those counted.
26.55: Nevertheless, the land shall be divided by lot. They shall inherit according to the names of their ancestral tribes.
26.56: By lot the possession shall be divided, whether between a larger or smaller group.
26.57: These are the Levites enrolled by their clans: from Gershon, the Gershonites; from Kohath, the Kohathites; from Merari, the Merarites.
26.58: These are the Levite clans: the Libnites, the Hebronites, the Mahlites, the Mushites, and the Korathites. Kohath fathered Amram.
26.59: The name of Amram's wife was Jochebed, daughter of Levi, who was born to Levi in Egypt. She bore to Amram: Aaron, Moses, and Miriam their sister.
26.60: Aaron's sons: Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.
26.61: Nadab and Abihu died when they offered unauthorized fire before the LORD.
26.62: Those numbered of the Levites — all males from a month old and upward — were 23,000. They were not enrolled among the Israelites because no inheritance was given to them among the Israelites.
26.63: These are those numbered by Moses and Eleazar the priest, who enrolled the Israelites in the plains of Moab beside the Jordan across from Jericho.
26.64: But not one of these was among those Moses and Aaron had registered when they counted the Israelites in the wilderness of Sinai.
26.65: For the LORD had said of them: They shall surely die in the wilderness. And not one of them was left except Caleb son of Jephunneh and Joshua son of Nun.

27.1: The daughters of Zelophehad son of Hepher, son of Gilead, son of Machir, son of Manasseh — from the clans of Manasseh son of Joseph — came forward. Their names: Mahlah, Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Tirzah.
27.2: They stood before Moses, Eleazar the priest, the leaders, and the whole congregation at the entrance of the tent of meeting, saying,
27.3: Our father died in the wilderness. He was not among those who joined against the LORD in Korah's company; he died for his own sin and had no sons.
27.4: Why should our father's name disappear from his clan just because he had no son? Give us a holding among our father's brothers.
27.5: Moses brought their case before the LORD.
27.6: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
27.7: The daughters of Zelophehad are right. You shall certainly give them a possession as an inheritance among their father's brothers; transfer their father's inheritance to them.
27.8: Speak to the Israelites: If a man dies and has no son, his inheritance shall pass to his daughter.
27.9: If he has no daughter, give his inheritance to his brothers.
27.10: If he has no brothers, give his inheritance to his father's brothers.
27.11: And if his father has no brothers, give his inheritance to the nearest relative in his family, and he shall take possession of it. This shall be a permanent legal statute for the Israelites, as the LORD commanded Moses.
27.12: The LORD said to Moses: Go up into these mountains of Abarim and see the land I have given to the Israelites.
27.13: When you have seen it, you too will be gathered to your people, as your brother Aaron was.
27.14: For you rebelled against my command in the wilderness of Zin, during the community's dispute, failing to uphold my holiness before their eyes at the waters — the waters of Meribah-Kadesh in the wilderness of Zin.
27.15: Moses spoke to the LORD, saying,
27.16: May the LORD, the God of the spirits of all flesh, appoint a man over the congregation
27.17: who will go out before them and come in before them, who will lead them out and bring them in — so that the congregation of the LORD will not be like sheep without a shepherd.
27.18: The LORD said to Moses: Take Joshua son of Nun, a man in whom the Spirit resides, and lay your hand on him.
27.19: Present him before Eleazar the priest and the whole congregation, and commission him in their sight.
27.20: Transfer some of your authority to him so that the whole congregation of Israelites will obey him.
27.21: He shall stand before Eleazar the priest, who shall inquire for him by the judgment of the Urim before the LORD. At his word they shall go out, and at his word they shall come in — he and all the Israelites, the whole congregation.
27.22: Moses did as the LORD commanded him. He took Joshua and presented him before Eleazar the priest and before the whole congregation.
27.23: He laid his hands on him and commissioned him, as the LORD had commanded through Moses.

28.1: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
28.2: Command the Israelites: My offering — the food of my fire offerings, a pleasing aroma to me — you shall be careful to present to me at their appointed times.
28.3: Say to them: This is the fire offering you shall present to the LORD — two yearling lambs without blemish each day, as a regular burnt offering.
28.4: Offer one lamb in the morning and the other at twilight,
28.5: with a grain offering of one-tenth of an ephah of fine flour mixed with one-fourth of a hin of pressed oil.
28.6: This is the regular burnt offering, instituted at Mount Sinai as a pleasing aroma — a fire offering to the LORD.
28.7: The drink offering for the first lamb: one-fourth of a hin. Pour the fermented drink as a drink offering to the LORD in the holy place.
28.8: Offer the second lamb at twilight with the same grain offering and drink offering as in the morning — a fire offering, a pleasing aroma to the LORD.
28.9: On the Sabbath day: two yearling lambs without blemish, a grain offering of two-tenths of an ephah of fine flour mixed with oil, and its drink offering.
28.10: This is the Sabbath burnt offering, in addition to the regular daily burnt offering and its drink offering.
28.11: At the beginning of each month, present a burnt offering to the LORD: two young bulls, one ram, and seven yearling lambs without blemish.
28.12: With each bull, a grain offering of three-tenths of an ephah of fine flour mixed with oil; with each ram, two-tenths of an ephah mixed with oil;
28.13: and one-tenth of an ephah of flour mixed with oil as a grain offering for each lamb — a burnt offering, a pleasing aroma, a fire offering to the LORD.
28.14: The drink offerings: half a hin of wine for each bull, one-third of a hin for each ram, one-fourth of a hin for each lamb. This is the monthly burnt offering for every month of the year.
28.15: One male goat shall be offered as a sin offering to the LORD, in addition to the regular burnt offering and its drink offering.
28.16: On the fourteenth day of the first month is the LORD's Passover.
28.17: On the fifteenth day of this month is the festival; for seven days unleavened bread shall be eaten.
28.18: On the first day there shall be a holy assembly; do not do any regular work.
28.19: But you shall present a fire offering as a burnt offering to the LORD: two bulls, one ram, seven yearling lambs — all without blemish.
28.20: Their grain offering: fine flour mixed with oil — three-tenths of an ephah for each bull and two-tenths for each ram.
28.21: Offer one-tenth for each of the seven lambs.
28.22: And one male goat as a sin offering to make atonement for you.
28.23: Offer these in addition to the morning burnt offering, which is the regular daily sacrifice.
28.24: In this manner, offer each day throughout the seven days the food of a fire offering — a pleasing aroma to the LORD — in addition to the regular burnt offering and its drink offering.
28.25: On the seventh day you shall have a holy assembly; do no regular work.
28.26: On the day of firstfruits, when you present a new grain offering to the LORD at your Festival of Weeks, you shall have a holy assembly; do no regular work.
28.27: Present a burnt offering for a pleasing aroma to the LORD: two bulls, one ram, seven yearling lambs.
28.28: With the grain offering: three-tenths of an ephah of flour mixed with oil for each bull, two-tenths for each ram,
28.29: One-tenth for each of the seven lambs.
28.30: One male goat to make atonement for you.
28.31: Offer these in addition to the regular burnt offering and its grain offering — they shall be without blemish — and with their drink offerings.

29.1: On the first day of the seventh month you shall have a holy assembly; do no regular work. It is a day of trumpet blasting for you.
29.2: Present a burnt offering as a pleasing aroma to the LORD: one bull, one ram, and seven yearling lambs without blemish.
29.3: Their grain offering of flour mixed with oil: three-tenths of an ephah for the bull and two-tenths for the ram,
29.4: And one-tenth for each of the seven lambs.
29.5: One male goat as a sin offering to make atonement for you.
29.6: These are in addition to the monthly burnt offering with its grain offering and the daily burnt offering with its grain offering and their drink offerings, according to their prescribed manner — a fire offering, a pleasing aroma to the LORD.
29.7: On the tenth day of this seventh month you shall have a holy assembly and shall afflict yourselves; you shall do no work.
29.8: Present a burnt offering to the LORD as a pleasing aroma: one bull, one ram, and seven yearling lambs — they shall be without blemish.
29.9: Their grain offering of flour mixed with oil: three-tenths for the bull and two-tenths for the ram,
29.10: One-tenth for each of the seven lambs.
29.11: One male goat as a sin offering, in addition to the sin offering of atonement and the regular burnt offering with its grain offering and their drink offerings.
29.12: On the fifteenth day of the seventh month you shall have a holy assembly; do no regular work, and celebrate a feast to the LORD for seven days.
29.13: Present a burnt offering, a fire offering, a pleasing aroma to the LORD: thirteen young bulls, two rams, and fourteen yearling lambs — they shall be without blemish.
29.14: Their grain offering of flour mixed with oil: three-tenths of an ephah for each of the thirteen bulls and two-tenths for each of the two rams,
29.15: And one-tenth for each of the fourteen lambs.
29.16: And one male goat as a sin offering, in addition to the regular burnt offering with its grain offering and drink offering.
29.17: On the second day: twelve young bulls, two rams, fourteen yearling lambs without blemish.
29.18: Their grain offerings and drink offerings for the bulls, the rams, and the lambs shall be according to their number, in the prescribed manner.
29.19: And one male goat as a sin offering, in addition to the regular burnt offering with its grain offering and drink offerings.
29.20: On the third day: eleven bulls, two rams, fourteen yearling lambs without blemish.
29.21: Their grain offerings and drink offerings for the bulls, the rams, and the lambs shall be according to their number, in the prescribed manner.
29.22: And one goat as a sin offering, in addition to the regular burnt offering with its grain offering and drink offering.
29.23: On the fourth day: ten bulls, two rams, and fourteen yearling lambs without blemish.
29.24: Their grain offerings and drink offerings for the bulls, the rams, and the lambs shall be according to their number, in the prescribed manner.
29.25: And one male goat as a sin offering, in addition to the regular burnt offering with its grain offering and drink offering.
29.26: On the fifth day: nine bulls, two rams, and fourteen yearling lambs without blemish.
29.27: Their grain offerings and drink offerings for the bulls, the rams, and the lambs shall be according to their number, in the prescribed manner.
29.28: And one goat as a sin offering, in addition to the regular burnt offering with its grain offering and drink offering.
29.29: On the sixth day: eight bulls, two rams, and fourteen yearling lambs without blemish.
29.30: Their grain offerings and drink offerings for the bulls, the rams, and the lambs shall be according to their number, in the prescribed manner.
29.31: And one goat as a sin offering, in addition to the regular burnt offering with its grain offering and drink offering.
29.32: On the seventh day: seven bulls, two rams, and fourteen yearling lambs without blemish.
29.33: Their grain offerings and drink offerings for the bulls, the rams, and the lambs shall be according to their number, in the prescribed manner.
29.34: And one goat as a sin offering, in addition to the regular burnt offering with its grain offering and drink offering.
29.35: On the eighth day you shall hold a closing assembly; do no regular work.
29.36: Present a burnt offering, a fire offering, a pleasing aroma to the LORD: one bull, one ram, seven yearling lambs without blemish.
29.37: Their grain offerings and drink offerings for the bull, the ram, and the lambs shall be according to their number, in the prescribed manner.
29.38: And one goat as a sin offering, in addition to the regular burnt offering with its grain offering and drink offering.
29.39: These are what you shall offer to the LORD at your appointed festivals, in addition to your vow offerings and freewill offerings — for your burnt offerings, grain offerings, drink offerings, and fellowship offerings.
29.40: Moses told the Israelites everything the LORD had commanded him.

30.1: Moses spoke to the heads of Israel's tribes, saying: This is what the LORD has commanded.
30.2: If a man makes a vow to the LORD or swears an oath to bind himself with a pledge, he must not break his word. He shall do everything he said.
30.3: If a woman makes a vow to the LORD and binds herself with a pledge while she is in her father's household as a young woman —
30.4: and her father hears of her vow and the pledge she bound upon herself, but says nothing — then all her vows and every pledge binding her shall stand.
30.5: But if her father disallows her on the day he hears of it, none of her vows or pledges shall stand. The LORD will forgive her, because her father disallowed her.
30.6: If she makes a vow or rashly pledges something with her lips that binds her, and then marries —
30.7: and her husband hears of it and says nothing to her on the day he hears — then her vows and the pledges binding her shall stand.
30.8: But if her husband disallows her on the day he hears it, he cancels the vow she made and whatever she pledged with her lips. The LORD will forgive her.
30.9: Every vow of a widow or a divorced woman — any pledge with which she has bound herself — shall stand.
30.10: If she made a vow or bound herself by a pledge with an oath while living in her husband's house —
30.11: and her husband heard it and said nothing and did not disallow her — then all her vows and every pledge she bound herself with shall stand.
30.12: But if her husband completely annulled them on the day he heard them, then whatever she spoke regarding her vows or the pledges of her soul shall not stand — her husband has annulled them, and the LORD will forgive her.
30.13: Every vow and every binding oath of self-denial — her husband may confirm it or her husband may annul it.
30.14: But if her husband says nothing to her from day to day, he confirms all her vows and pledges on her — he ratifies them, because he said nothing to her on the day he first heard them.
30.15: But if he annuls them after he has heard them, he shall bear her guilt.
30.16: These are the statutes the LORD commanded Moses concerning the relationship between a man and his wife, and between a father and his daughter while she is still young and in her father's house.
